title: EV Operations Engineer
description: **The Role:** Join a fast\-growing tech company delivering EV charging solutions across Ireland. This role blends hardware, software, and customer support, giving you hands\-on exposure to connected devices and real\-world infrastructure. **Responsibilities:** * Provide **1st and 2nd line operations support** across EV charging hardware and software platforms * Troubleshoot issues related to **hardware, connectivity, networking, and user access** * Manage incoming support tickets via ticketing systems * Monitor live systems and proactively respond to alerts or faults * Support **remote diagnostics** , onboarding, and customer issue resolution * Coordinate with internal engineering teams and third\-party partners to escalate complex issues * Manage and support EV chargers installed at customer homes and commercial sites * Occasionally travel to customer sites to diagnose and resolve technical issues * Maintain accurate documentation and contribute to the internal knowledge base * Take full ownership and accountability for customer issues from start to resolution **Required:** * **Experience** in technical support, IT support, or field support roles * Degree in **Electrical Engineering, IT or a related field** * Strong troubleshooting skills across **hardware and software environments** * Experience using ticketing systems. * Excellent communication skills with a strong **customer\-first mindset** * Professional, outgoing personality with strong interpersonal skills * Knowledge in EV, IoT, telecoms, or energy sector is a plus *** Full, clean driver’s licence**

title: Electrical Engineers – Upskill for the Energy Transmission & Distribution Sector
description: **Company Description** **Work with Us. Change the World.** At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world's most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of nearly 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** **Start Here. Grow Here.** **Electrical Engineers – Upskill for the Energy Transmission \& Distribution Sector** Are you an Electrical Engineer looking to move into a new sector? Do you feel limited by your current industry or unsure how to transition your skills into a growing market? At AECOM, we recognise that qualified electrical engineers bring highly transferable technical expertise, problem\-solving capability, and experience working on complex engineering systems. Whether your background is in water, transportation, buildings, manufacturing, consultancy, or another engineering discipline, we can support your move into the Energy Transmission \& Distribution (T\&D) sector. **We are offering a unique development opportunity for Electrical Engineers to join our growing UK \& Ireland T\&D team.** Working alongside experienced industry specialists, you will receive structured mentoring, technical guidance, and access to industry\-leading training to help you build expertise across: * Transmission \& Distribution Systems * Transmission Cables * Substations * Grid Infrastructure * HV Power Systems You will also have access to AECOM University and our extensive internal technical networks to support your professional development. Opportunities are available across our UK \& Ireland offices, \- **Glasgow, Edinburgh, Newcastle, Manchester \& Dublin** — while also benefiting from our flexible hybrid working model. **Why Join AECOM?** **Impact That Matters** Work on nationally significant energy infrastructure projects that support the UK \& Ireland’s transition to a low\-carbon future while improving communities and protecting the environment. **Award\-Winning Excellence** Join an industry\-leading team delivering innovative engineering solutions across major transmission and distribution programmes. **Career Development** Whether you are early in your career or already progressing toward Chartership, you’ll benefit from: * Structured mentoring * Technical practice networks * AECOM University * Leadership development opportunities * Support from experienced T\&D professionals **Energy at AECOM** At AECOM, we are helping lead the transformation of the energy sector toward a more sustainable and inclusive future. As the UK and Europe accelerate the transition to clean energy and net zero carbon targets, we are delivering innovative low\-carbon energy solutions that meet the needs of industry, communities, and the environment. **Transmission \& Distribution (T\&D)** The journey to achieving 2030 net zero targets requires major investment in energy infrastructure, including: * New overhead transmission lines * Underground cable systems * Substations * Battery storage facilities * HVDC and subsea interconnectors AECOM’s Transmission \& Distribution team is supporting the network development and grid modernisation needed to deliver this transition — and you could be part of it. **Come Grow With Us** Our T\&D Engineering team delivers services across the full project lifecycle, including: * High Voltage Power Systems Analysis * Cabling * Substations * Transmission \& Distribution Infrastructure * Protection Systems * SCADA \& Communications * System Balancing \& Compensation * Converter Station Design * HVDC Systems * Interconnectors \& Overseas Links **Working to Modernise the UK’s Energy Infrastructure** AECOM has recently been appointed, in joint venture with Arup, to provide design and consenting services for National Grid’s Great Grid Upgrade programme. This major infrastructure investment will strengthen the UK electricity transmission network, helping connect renewable energy sources — including offshore wind — into the grid by 2030 and beyond. The programme will support: * Grid modernisation * Increased energy resilience * Reduced dependence on fossil fuels * Delivery of the UK’s net zero ambitions **Start Here. Grow Here.** Are you ready to join a highly experienced, growing, industry\-leading Transmission \& Distribution team and develop your career on transformational projects that will help deliver the energy transition? At AECOM, you’ll thrive in an inclusive and forward\-thinking environment where your contribution is valued. And let's not forget about the perks at AECOM. You'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, customised to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, our flexible hybrid working model to ensure a work\-life balance that suits your lifestyle, technical practice networks, AECOM University, and volunteering days. **Qualifications** **Ready to Push the Limits of What’s Possible?** We are looking for candidates with: * An Electrical or Electronic Engineering qualification such as: * BSc/BEng * MSc/MEng * Demonstrable electrical engineering design and/or consultancy experience in any regulated sector * Strong communication skills with the ability to engage confidently with clients, colleagues, stakeholders, and subcontractors * The ability to explain, discuss, and resolve technical issues effectively while capturing and responding to feedback **Additional Information** **Ready to Make the Move?** Join our world\-class Energy team and help shape the future of Transmission \& Distribution. Apply today. We celebrate diversity, including neurodiversity, and believe it strengthens our teams. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and abilities. If you require reasonable accommodation during the application process, please contact: ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com Interested in learning more about life at AECOM? Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to hear employee stories, explore our projects, and discover more about our culture. For further information about the role, please connect with the recruiter on LinkedIn: Anne Marie Flynn \| LinkedIn **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodationsUKI@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. **ReqID:** REF59120V **Business Line:** Energy **Business Group:** DCS **Strategic Business Unit:** Europe \& India **Career Area:** Engineering **Work Location Model:** Hybrid

title: Enterprise Account Executive
description: **Why join us** TRACTIAN is transforming the industrial world by empowering frontline maintenance workers to achieve more. We’ve fused cutting\-edge hardware with innovative software into one powerful platform, disrupting legacy systems and delivering smarter, faster solutions for our clients. At TRACTIAN, you'll break boundaries, question convention, and collaborate with top talent to drive real change. As a part of our growth\-stage startup, you’ll work alongside the founders, shaping the vision, products, and experiences that will define the future of industrial tech. **Sales at TRACTIAN** The Sales team is the driving force behind revenue at Tractian, generating new opportunities, acquiring top\-tier customers like Hyundai, Bosch, and Kraft Heinz, and strengthening relationships with our current accounts. Backed by strategic investors with a track record of building unicorns, Tractian is poised to set new benchmarks in industrial technology. Recognized on the Forbes AI 50 list in 2024 and ranked in the 98th percentile by RepVue for inbound leads, we deliver undeniable value—boosting machine reliability, delivering immediate ROI, and achieving world\-class revenue retention that matches the best in tech. At Tractian, top performers are recognized, rewarded, and empowered to overachieve their goals. **What You'll Do** As an Enterprise Account Executive at Tractian, you will be responsible for driving revenue growth through the acquisition of new enterprise customers and the expansion of existing accounts across your assigned territory. You will build and manage long\-term relationships with key stakeholders, identify opportunities for upsell and cross\-sell, and ensure strong customer satisfaction throughout the sales lifecycle. You will own your pipeline from prospecting through close, contribute to regional revenue targets, and collaborate closely with Sales Engineering, Customer Success, and Product teams to support Tractian’s growth in the European market. **Responsibilities** * Own new enterprise logo acquisition within an assigned territory, with a primary focus on outbound prospecting and pipeline generation. * Drive net\-new revenue by identifying, engaging, and converting prospective enterprise customers through structured outbound efforts, including targeted outreach and cold calling. * Develop and execute territory and account strategies to build a strong pipeline of qualified enterprise opportunities. * Lead the full sales cycle for new business opportunities, from initial prospecting through negotiation and close. * Use HubSpot CRM to manage pipeline activity, forecast revenue accurately, and track progress against new business targets. * Collaborate closely with Sales Engineering, Customer Success, and Product teams to position solutions effectively for complex enterprise buying environments. * Conduct high\-level discovery with senior stakeholders to understand operational challenges and position Tractian’s solutions as long\-term strategic investments. * Negotiate and close complex enterprise agreements, balancing short\-term wins with sustainable, long\-term customer value. , **Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Business, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience. * 5\+ years of experience in enterprise B2B sales, with a demonstrated track record of net\-new customer acquisition and revenue growth. * Proven ability to build pipeline through outbound prospecting, including cold outreach, strategic targeting, and multi\-stakeholder engagement. * Strong understanding of complex B2B sales cycles, enterprise buying processes, and consultative selling methodologies. * Experience using CRM tools (e.g., HubSpot) to manage pipeline, forecast revenue, and track sales activity with discipline and accuracy. * Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder\-management skills, including engagement with senior decision\-makers. * Strategic, results\-driven mindset with the ability to operate independently in a high\-growth, target\-oriented environment. * Ability to manage multiple opportunities simultaneously while maintaining strong attention to detail and execution quality. **Compensation:** * Full\-time employment with all mandatory statutory social and health insurance * Paid vacation: 20 days annually, plus national holidays * Pension Scheme, 1,5% match * International travel for company kick\-off events at our headquarters in Atlanta, GA, United States * Sports Incentive – Monthly bonus for regular participation in physical activities * Long\-Term Benefit – After four years of service, enjoy a fully funded trip anywhere in the world

title: European FX Sales
description: **Job Description** European FX Sales At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of FX Sales to join our BNY Markets European FX team. This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Managing all aspects of existing FX client relationships across the European Union. * Delivering high quality execution across the FX product suite; including spot, forwards, swaps, options. * Contributing towards the growth of BNY’s FX footprint within the European Union. * Ensuring all FX trading activity is conducted within our regulatory \& compliance framework. To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * University level qualified. * A genuine enthusiasm for financial markets. * Ability to work under pressure and in a fast\-paced environment. * Proof of an extremely high level of attention to detail and organizational ability. * Fluency in French or another European language beneficial, but not compulsory. At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn **Here’s a Few Of Our Recent Awards** * America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 * “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 **Our Benefits And Rewards** BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Executive Assistant
description: **About the role** You will play a key role in supporting our Chief People Officer, acting as a trusted partner to enable them to operate at pace and with maximum impact. As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CPO operates at the heart of the organisation, and this role will provide critical support in navigating that environment. This is a highly visible position, sitting at the centre of the People function, where you will also provide administrative and coordination support to the broader HR leadership team. You will work closely with other Executive Assistants and senior stakeholders across the business to ensure alignment, seamless coordination, and effective communication at the executive level. You’ll be responsible for ensuring the smooth running of day\-to\-day activities, while also contributing to the organisation and delivery of key People initiatives. This includes managing complex scheduling, preparing high\-quality presentations and materials, coordinating leadership meetings, and supporting the planning and delivery of internal events. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide proactive, day\-to\-day support to the Chief People Officer, ensuring priorities are managed effectively and time is optimised * Manage complex scheduling across multiple time zones, including meetings, travel, and key People and Executive Leadership forums * Coordinate closely with Executive Assistants to the wider Executive Leadership Team to ensure alignment of priorities, meetings, and communications * Support the wider HR leadership team where required with administrative coordination, diary management, and meeting logistics * Prepare high\-quality presentations, reports, and briefing materials for executive and board\-level discussions * Coordinate board and management packs, ensuring accuracy and timely delivery * Support the planning and delivery of internal events, offsites, and leadership sessions * Act as a central point of coordination across HR, tracking progress, managing deadlines, and following up on actions * Monitor project status and produce clear, concise reporting for leadership visibility * Liaise with internal teams and external partners to support operational processes, including vendor management and payments * Act as a key point of contact for senior stakeholders, both internally and externally, handling queries with professionalism and discretion * Identify potential risks, bottlenecks, or delays and work proactively with stakeholders to resolve them * Leverage systems and emerging technologies to improve efficiency, streamline processes, and enhance ways of working **About you** * Proven track record as a successful Executive Assistant in a global organization. * Excellent communication skills, written and verbal at all levels. * Exceptional time\-management/organisational skills, and ability to produce high\-quality work. * A driven and adaptable team player willing to go the extra mile and rise to any challenge. * Ability to work well under pressure and manage multiple priorities. * High levels of professionalism, practices strict confidentiality with all materials, and exercises discretion when interfacing with the business – familiarity with confidential HR matters is a bonus. * Confident in working independently, problem\-solving and developing ideas. * Strong business and commercial acumen. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office with aptitude to learn new software and systems. **About Inizio** At Inizio, we're a global team driven by a shared purpose: to reimagine health. Formed in 2022 through the coming together of Ashfield and Huntsworth, we unite decades of experience with cutting\-edge capabilities to deliver impactful solutions across the commercialization journey \- from discovery to post\-launch. Our global network of over 10,000 experts operates across 50 countries, supporting clients with strategic insight, medical excellence, creative engagement, and real\-world delivery. We work with the world's leading health and life sciences businesses to solve complex challenges and drive meaningful outcomes for our clients and patients. What sets Inizio apart is our people \- and we believe everyone should have the opportunity to thrive. Here, you're encouraged to reimagine your possibilities: to grow, to be curious and to make real change happen. It's a place where your individuality is valued, your development is supported, and your ambition is celebrated. If you're ready to push boundaries and accelerate your career, Inizio is the best place to be. **Our Pledge** At Inizio, we value inclusivity, recognize the power of diversity and inspire the next generation of change\-makers. We are an equal opportunities employer. We believe in creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity, equity and inclusion. We aim to recruit from a diverse slate of candidates and foster an environment that provides the right conditions for long\-term success. We welcome all applications regardless of race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, marital status, military veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation and physical or mental disability. *Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.*

title: Executive Assistant - Corporate M&A (12-month FTC)
description: ### **Description** Arthur Cox LLP is one of Ireland’s leading law firms. We are an “all\-island” firm with offices in Dublin and Belfast. We also have offices in London, New York, and San Francisco. Our practice encompasses all aspects of corporate and business law. With 600 legal staff and a total headcount of nearly 900, we provide a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks, and financial institutions and established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, we have led the way in the legal field in Ireland. For our clients, we work to deliver the best service and support. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. **Department: Corporate M\&A Team** **Location: Dublin (3/4 days onsite)** **Reports to: Practice Support Manager** **Contract Type: Full\-time / 12\-month FTC** **Background:** We are looking for a well\-organised and motivated Executive Assistant to join our dynamicCorporate M\&A Department. This is an excellent opportunity for someone in their legal career to gain hands\-on experience in a fast\-paced and technical area of law. The successful candidate will provide administrative and secretarial support to a team of lawyers. **About the Role:** This role demands a high level of attention to detail. You’ll work closely with your Practice Support Manager, the EA team and Fee Earners within the Corporate M\&A team.### **Key Responsibilities** **Personal Assistant \& Legal Support:*** Provide high\-level administrative assistance. * Speed, responsiveness and efficiency are key for this role. * Supporting partners and associates within the team and supporting EA holiday cover, sick leave. **Travel Coordination:*** Proactively manage complex travel schedules, including flights, accommodations, and itineraries. * Ensure all travel arrangements align with partner preferences. * Prepare travel\-related documentation and meeting materials. * Booking conferences. Maintaining and updating client marketing database (Interaction). * **Expense Management:*** Prepare and submit detailed expense reports in a timely manner. * Track and reconcile expenses against budgets and client billing. Ensure compliance with firm policies and client reimbursement guidelines * **3E Billing \& Time Entry:*** Managing AML requirements for client onboarding, ensuring all procedures are followed. * Running conflict checks and drafting Engagement Letters. * Managing billing processes to include time entry, invoicing, client account balances and follow up on outstanding payments. * Managing and inputting timesheets on a daily basis. * Managing audit/accrual requests for clients. * Liaise with the billing department to review and edit pro formas. Monitor billing cycles and ensure timely invoicing and payment follow\-ups * **Client \& Team Interaction:*** Serve as a point of contact for clients, maintaining professional relationships. * Coordinate with internal teams including marketing, finance, and HR. * Support business development efforts, including CRM updates and client event coordination. ### **Skills Knowledge and Expertise** * Minimum 3 years' experience supporting senior legal professionals. * Proficiency in MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook). * Experience with legal billing software (3E). * Experience with BigHand. * Excellent attention to detail on travel arrangements and coordinating travel itineraries. * Excellent judgment, discretion, and attention to detail. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. * Excellent organisation skills with the ability to prioritise workload. * Team player, who can also work on their own initiative. * Excellent oral and written communication skills. ### **Benefits** We reward, encourage and support our talented people to reach their full potential offering excellent progression opportunities along with a market leading remuneration package and benefits including:* Competitive salary * 25 days annual leave * Immediate pension scheme * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits including full gym and wellness programme * TaxSaver and Bike to Work scheme * Wide\-ranging opportunities for involvement in internal committees ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Executive Assistant- Global Tech
description: Executive Assistant \& Operations Coordinator A globally recognised technology organisation is seeking an organised and proactive Executive Assistant \& Operations Coordinator to join their Dublin\-based team on an 8\-month maternity cover contract.This is a varied position supporting senior leadership within a fast\-paced international environment. The role will suit someone who enjoys working across executive support, team coordination, operations, and day\-to\-day business support while managing multiple priorities in a dynamic setting. **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide executive support including calendar management, meeting coordination, travel arrangements, and expense management * Coordinate team meetings, offsites, events, and internal logistics * Support operational reporting, documentation, and administrative processes * Assist with budget tracking and team coordination activities * Manage internal communications, permissions, and distribution lists * Support cross\-functional collaboration across international teams * Act as a key point of contact for internal stakeholders and team coordination * Maintain accurate records and ensure smooth day\-to\-day operations **Requirements:** * 3\+ years’ experience in an Executive Assistant, Team Coordinator, Operations Coordinator, or Senior Administration role * Experience supporting managers or leadership teams * Strong organisational and multitasking skills * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities * Experience working in fast\-paced corporate or multinational environments * Strong attention to detail and ability to prioritise effectively * Proficient across Microsoft Office applications * Ability to work proactively and independently **Package:** * €40,000 salary * 8\-month fixed\-term contract * Fully onsite role based in Dublin 2 * Opportunity to join a globally recognised technology organisation Interested in hearing more? For a confidential discussion, contact Emma O’Connell on \+353 1 632 5032 or email your CV to emma.oconnell@recruiters.ie

title: Fast Food Cook Full Time
description: **We’re Hiring – Full\-Time Chef** Swingyard Driving Range, Elmgreen Golf Centre Join the Team at Swingyard – Part of the Carr Golf Group We’re looking for a skilled, efficient Chef to join our kitchen at Swingyard, Elmgreen Golf Centre, Dublin 15 – a newly renovated, high\-tech golf entertainment venue. This isn’t your typical driving range. With in\-bay dining, a licensed café, outdoor terrace, and cutting\-edge ball\-tracking tech, we offer a fun, interactive experience for all ages. As Chef, you’ll prepare and cook a variety of dishes quickly and to a high standard, ensuring a great food experience for our guests. **Key Responsibilities** * Receive and Prepare Orders: Take customer orders from waitstaff and prepare food according to specific requests. * Cooking: Grill, cook, and fry a variety of foods including hamburgers, sandwiches, pizzas, fries and other quick\-serve items. * Order Management: Plan and manage work orders to ensure all components of an order are ready and served simultaneously. * Food Preparation: Perform basic food preparation tasks, such as slicing meats and vegetables, and garnishing dishes. * Service: Complete orders ensuring it is presented attractively before served to customers and ensure it is presented attractively before serving to customers. * Inventory Management: Order supplies as needed and ensure shelves are well\-stocked. * Cleaning: Maintain cleanliness of food preparation equipment and areas, adhering to health and safety standards. * Wastage: Ensuring that a minimum of food is wasted. **Requirements** * Previous experience as a short\-order cook or in a similar role. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. * Excellent communication skills. * Basic knowledge of food safety and hygiene practices * Fully HACCP Trained would be an advantage but if not, full training will be given. * Flexibility to work various shifts, including weekends and holidays **What We Offer!** * 20 Days Annual Leave * 1 Additional Annual Leave Day for your Birthday * Additional Annual Leave with Length of Service * Complimentary rounds of golf * Discounts in Pro Shop * Car Parking On\-Site * Social Club * Annual Salary Review * Cycle to Work Scheme Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Field Sales Intern – EV Charging Infrastructure

Our commitment is to expedite the adoption of sustainable transportation by delivering dependable, scalable, and user\-friendly EV charging solutions throughout India. Internship Overview We are in search of enthusiastic and driven Field Sales Interns to become part of our team in Bangalore. This internship offers practical experience in the dynamic EV charging sector, with a clear pathway to a permanent position for exceptional performers. **Qualifications** * Currently enrolled in a Business, Marketing, or related field program; recent graduates are also welcome to apply. * Proficient in verbal and written communication. * Familiarity with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word). * Capable of working independently and collaboratively within a team. * Self\-motivated with a keen interest in learning about the sales process. * Knowledge of CRM software is advantageous but not mandatory. **Skills** * Exceptional interpersonal skills with the ability to establish connections with clients. * Strong organizational abilities and attention to detail. * Resilience in handling rejection and maintaining a positive outlook in challenging scenarios * Adaptability to fast\-paced environments and shifting priorities. **Requirements** Key Responsibilities * Promote and sell Tecell’s EV charging solutions to potential clients, including businesses, property developers, and fleet operators. * Conduct field visits to identify new business opportunities and establish relationships with prospective customers. * Demonstrate and explain the features and benefits of our EV charging products and services. * Collect and analyze customer feedback, market trends, and competitor activities. * Assist in organizing and participating in promotional events, exhibitions, and product demonstrations. * Prepare and submit daily/weekly sales reports and maintain accurate records of sales activities. * Collaborate with the sales and technical teams to ensure customer satisfaction and successful project implementation. Candidate Profile * Education: + Minimum: 12th pass (Higher Secondary) with at least 2 years of sales experience + OR Bachelor’s degree in any discipline (freshers or experienced) * Experience: + Minimum 2 years in sales (for non\-graduates) + Sales experience in infrastructure, automotive, or technology sectors is an advantage * Skills: + Strong communication and interpersonal skills + Self\-motivated, target\-driven, and eager to learn + Ability to work independently and as part of a team + Willingness to travel extensively within Bangalore and surrounding areas Internship Details * Duration: 3 to 6 months * Stipend: Competitive; includes allowances for travel and food during field sales duties * Performance\-Based Opportunity: Successful interns will be offered a permanent position upon satisfactory completion of the internship **Benefits** \*\*What We Offer:\*\* * Real\-world exposure to the rapidly growing EV industry * Mentorship from experienced professionals * Opportunity to build a rewarding career in sustainable technology * Allowances for travel and food during field assignments check(event) ; career\-website\-detail\-template\-2 \=\> apply(record.id,meta)" mousedown\="lyte\-button \=\> check(event)" final\-style\="background\-color:\#6875E2;border\-color:\#6875E2;color:white;" final\-class\="lyte\-button lyteBackgroundColorBtn lyteSuccess" lyte\-rendered\=""\>

title: Field Sales Rep - Belfast
description: **The Team** The Regional UK and Ireland team drives Deliveroo's performance across all 4 nations of UKI. From Brighton to London to Bognor to Belfast, we are experts in the local markets we serve. As part of the regional UKI team, you will sign up the best restaurants in the city/region. Regional UKI presents one of the biggest growth opportunities available to Deliveroo and we are building a team of commercially experienced, individuals to help us fulfil our potential. You will work within the Belfast region and report into the Regional Manager. **The Role** This is a great opportunity to become an important member of a growing business in a competitive market. You'll build relationships with new restaurant partners in your area, improving our proposition for customers and identifying ways to continue our fast growth. This is an excellent role for someone who enjoys building relationships. **Please note a valid driving licence and a car is required for the role and we are looking for candidates who are happy to travel to different areas around Belfast. This is not a typical office job and therefore being out on the road must be something you're comfortable with.** **What You'll Be Doing** * Map, prospect, pitch and close new restaurants across your region in a very organised fashion. This means being a city\-level expert including knowing the up\-and\-coming restaurants and which restaurants customers crave most * Communicate (physical visits, emails, calls, and social media) with restaurants to demonstrate Deliveroo technology * Oversee the restaurant onboarding process to ensure it is as quick, efficient and seamless as possible * Ensure restaurant success within the first eight weeks from signing * Sample food from a variety of high\-quality establishments * Attend restaurant and food meet\-ups **KPIs** * Number of restaurants signed each month * Performance of restaurants signed (first 3 months) **Required Skills** * Be comfortable with targeting new business and hunting out hidden gems within Northern Ireland * Experienced negotiator and able to structure win\-win deals for restaurants and for Deliveroo * Commercially knowledgeable who understands the economics of food delivery \- for restaurants and Deliveroo * Have great communication, whether this be face\-to\-face, through phone or email * Be able to demonstrate product and industry knowledge to clients * Take ownership and work within demanding targets * Have an interest in all things food and restaurants * Be tenacious and motivated **Why Deliveroo** Our mission is to transform the way you shop and eat, bringing the neighbourhood to your door by connecting consumers, restaurants, shops and riders. We are transforming the way the world eats and shops by making access to food and products more convenient and enjoyable. We give people the opportunity to buy what they want, as they want it, when and where they want it. We are a technology\-driven company at the forefront of the most rapidly expanding industry in the world. We are still a small team, making a very large impact, looking to answer some of the most interesting questions out there. We move fast, value autonomy and ownership, and we are always looking for new ideas. **Workplace \& Benefits** At Deliveroo we know that people are the heart of the business and we prioritise their welfare. Benefits differ by country, but we offer many benefits in areas including healthcare, well\-being, parental leave, pensions, and generous annual leave allowances, including time off to support a charitable cause of your choice. Benefits are country\-specific, please ask your recruiter for more information. **Diversity** At Deliveroo, we believe a great workplace is one that represents the world we live in and how beautifully diverse it can be. That means we have no judgement when it comes to any one of the things that make you who you are \- your gender, race, sexuality, religion or a secret aversion to coriander. All you need is a passion for (most) food and a desire to be part of one of the fastest\-growing businesses in a rapidly growing industry. We are committed to diversity, equity and inclusion in all aspects of our hiring process. We recognise that some candidates may require adjustments to apply for a position or fairly participate in the interview process. If you require any adjustments, please don't hesitate to let us know. We will make every effort to provide the necessary adjustments to ensure you have an equitable opportunity to succeed.

title: Field Technician & Operations Associate
description: **Speed Queen Self Service Laundry – Ireland \& UK** **Location:** Dublin (WeWork, 2 Dublin Landings) \+ travel across Ireland **Type:** Full\-time **About Us** Speed Queen Self Service Laundry is part of DNL Collective, a growing group focused on building and scaling operationally strong, multi\-site businesses across Ireland and the UK. Our laundry division is one of the fastest\-growing self\-service operators in Europe, with 40\+ locations and continued expansion underway into the UK. We build and operate high\-quality launderette locations with a focus on reliability, efficiency, and customer experience. As we scale, we’re looking for ambitious individuals who want to grow with the business and take on real responsibility early. **The Role** This is a hands\-on, fast\-paced role combining field\-based technical work with operational and project support. You’ll split your time between: * Working on\-site across our launderette locations (installations, maintenance, troubleshooting) * Supporting the rollout of new stores * Coordinating operational tasks and projects You’ll work closely with the Operations team and Senior Technician, gaining exposure to both technical problem\-solving and how a multi\-site business is run and scaled. This role is ideal for someone practical, organised, and eager to learn quickly in a growing business. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Assist with the installation, maintenance, and repair of laundry equipment across multiple sites * Carry out basic troubleshooting and fault finding (full training provided) * Work alongside the Senior Technician to diagnose and resolve issues * Develop the ability to independently handle technical fixes over time **Operations \& Project Support** * Support new store fit\-outs and openings * Track equipment orders and manage inventory of machines and parts * Help maintain project timelines and track progress * Assist with issue resolution across multiple locations * Maintain accurate records and reporting using internal systems **General** * Travel to sites across Ireland (typically between 2\-4 days per week depending on projects) * Spend 2–3 days per week in the Dublin office (WeWork, 2 Dublin Landings) * Support the wider operations team in a fast\-moving environment **What We’re Looking For** * Practical, hands\-on mindset with an interest in technical work * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Problem\-solving attitude and willingness to learn quickly * Comfortable using digital tools and systems, particularly Microsoft Excel for tracking, * reporting, and operational tasks * Self\-starter who can take initiative and work independently * Flexible and adaptable in a fast\-moving environment * Full, clean driving licence (required) * Willingness to travel across Ireland * Experience in a technical, mechanical, or operations role is a plus, but not required. **What We Offer** * €33,000 – €40,000 \+ company van (depending on experience) * Full training on equipment, systems, and troubleshooting * Direct exposure to senior leadership and experienced technical staff * Clear progression into Senior Technician or Operations roles * Opportunity to be part of a business scaling across Ireland and into the UK * A fast\-paced, supportive environment with real responsibility from day one Pay: €33,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: FinOps Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who We Are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What We Do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our People** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose** The FinOps Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education \& Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations

title: Financial Accountant
description: Financial Accountant, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope Of The Role** The right candidate will support our journey to streamline financial and operational processes, including implementation of new systems \& processes to accommodate rapid growth and have outstanding communication and interpersonal skills. You must be a driven, high energy, customer service focused individual that can work well with internal and external stakeholders at all levels, a critical thinker who can drive projects and make an impact. You have exceptional collaboration and communication skills and a desire to help drive business goals in a matrix organization. You are a process improver that can easily pick up the high\-level business goals and apply them to the finance and accounting operations. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Reporting to the GL Leads * General Ledger team member duties + Month\-end \& Year\-end closure of accounts + Ensure data entered and postings made are on\-time and accurate across all key areas such as revenue, costs, FX, prepayments, accruals, and all areas covering the P\&L and Balance Sheet + Detailed review before, during and after each step is completed to ensure the highest level of quality. + Ensure policies and procedures are followed and that all control steps are completed. * Supporting elements of the year\-end audit both at group and local levels as well as internal audit queries * Support the Identification and delivery of solutions that will enhance and automate existing processes. Challenge the current approach, offer views on improvement, support implementation of those improvements. * Support the on\-going work to develop and document business processes and accounting policies to maintain and strengthen internal controls. * Create strong business partnerships within your own team and across all teams in the business. Be a team player, success in this role is based on working with others throughout the business. * Key callout, Mercury is on a journey to strengthen its financial reporting processes, controls, and team structure, this is a key focus. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Qualified accountant (ACA/ACCA or equivalent) with a minimum 3 years’ experience in multinational environment. * Experience in using SAP and managing month\-end \& other core General Ledger related activities is essential. * Relevant industry PQE with large, fast\-growing, multi\-national company. * Very organised and thorough with desire for continuous improvement, attention to detail * Strong foundation in Financial Accounting compliance with experience of initiating and driving projects to completion. * Proven ability to multitask, with ability to design plans and deliver against them. * Strong IT and analytical skills * Experienced in Microsoft Suite with strong skills in Excel (SAP BW and PowerBi experience would also be useful) * Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal skills. * Self\-starter with a drive to deliver high quality work, on\-time while adding value. ***Mercury is an Equal Opportunity Employer.*** Apply Now

title: Financial Accountant
description: **The Role** We’re looking for a **qualified, hands** ‑ **on accountant** to join our finance. This is a broad role with **end** ‑ **to** ‑ **end ownership** of core finance activities across a multi‑entity group, plus exposure to leadership and Board‑level reporting. You’ll suit this role if you enjoy responsibility, can work independently, and like using financial insight to influence business decisions, not just report on them. This role will report to the Head of Finance over the long term, with an initial period providing maternity leave coverage for that role. We are **open to considering any candidates who would like a flexible/part time working pattern** \[3–5 days per week]. **What You’ll Do** * Own **month** ‑ **end** and management accounts across 4 entities * Deliver clear **variance analysis** and balance sheet control * Review AP, AR and bank postings; support and guide junior team members * Support **budgeting, forecasting** and performance tracking * Prepare monthly management packs and bi‑monthly **Board reports** * Prepare and submit **VAT returns** (Ireland \& UK) * Coordinate year‑end processes with external accountants * Contribute to process improvements and ad‑hoc commercial analysis * Contribute to pricing and profitability strategies **Influence \& Exposure** * This role works closely with senior leadership. You’ll contribute to discussions on performance, risk, cash flow and priorities; translating financial data into insight that supports decision‑making. * To do this well, you’ll need strong judgement, confidence explaining financial implications to non‑finance stakeholders, and a solid grasp of the business behind the numbers. **About You** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA) * Industry background preferred but not mandatory * Strong experience in month‑end, financial reporting and balance sheet control * Comfortable working independently and reviewing others’ work * Analytical, detail‑focused and commercially minded * Clear communicator with non‑finance stakeholders **Why Strata** **Recognition \& credibility** * Engineering Company of the Year 2025 (Irish Construction Industry Awards). * A culture of excellence backed by ISO 9001, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, ISO 27001 and ISO 19650 accreditations. **Purpose\-led** * B Corp certified and part of a movement focused on high standards of social, environmental and ethical performance. **Development that’s not lip service** * Structured performance and continuous professional development planning. * Support for ongoing learning and continued education. **Competitive compensation package** * Competitive salary with annual reviews. * Pension scheme with matching contributions. **Benefits \& flexibility** * 25 days annual leave (plus bank holidays). * Paid maternity, paternity and adoptive leave . * Hybrid and flexible working options. * Wellbeing supports including Wellchoice wellness benefit and EAP. * Death in service cover. * Taxsaver commuter tickets, bike to work scheme. * Culture and social initiatives. **Equal Opportunity \& Inclusion** At Strata, we’re committed to building an inclusive workplace where different perspectives are valued and people feel supported to do their best work. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, or would like to discuss flexible working options, please let us know \- we’re happy to support.

title: Financial Accountant (Newly Qualified)
description: **About The Role** **Who Are We?** At Panda, Ireland's largest Waste Management business, we invite you to join our Team and experience a career that champions environmental excellence while harmonising tradition and innovation. Be part of a team that values strong connections and supports one another. At Panda, we are committed to leading the way in waste management and recycling solutions. Embrace our innovative spirit as we work together to preserve our environment and create a greener future for generations to come. **Now Let Us Tell You About The Role** * Reporting of divisional Profit \& Loss Accounts within month end deadlines * Responsible for the production and reconciliation of all relevant Balance Sheet accounts. * Preparation of month end journals re accruals and prepayments * Reporting explanation of P\&L variances Vs budget * Assisting P\&L owners with understanding of results and variances Vs budget * Ensure all assigned g/l reconciliations are prepared on a timely basis within set deadlines. * Identify and escalate any problems/issues with reconciliations as they occur. * Recommend any improvements in procedures etc to improve efficiency of completing tasks. * Year End Statutory Reporting and Bank obligation reporting * ACA / ACCA Qualification **What We Are Looking For** * ACA / ACCA Qualified * Previous experience in financial accounting or audit environment * Good understanding of financial reporting and month end processes * Experience preparing balance sheet reconciliations and journals * Ability to analyse financial information and explain variances * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Ability to work to deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Good communication and stakeholder management skills * Experience with ERP/accounting systems would be an advantage * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Positive and proactive approach to work **About You** About Us **We are Panda. We value waste.** We’re in the business of waste management but that doesn’t just mean collecting bins. Behind every collection is a passionate team of people finding smarter, safer, and more sustainable ways to recycle and repurpose materials that would otherwise go to waste. Over the past 30 years, Panda has grown into a leading resource recovery business, bringing together almost 3,000 people across multiple brands, united by a shared vision and strong values. While our operations are diverse, our purpose is clear: to protect our people, support our customers, and create positive impact for the communities and environments we serve. At Panda, **people are at the heart of everything we do** . We work as one team, combining different skills, experiences, and perspectives to achieve shared goals. We care for each other’s wellbeing, encourage development, and support growth because we know we’re stronger together. We expect **excellence** in how we operate. High standards, bold thinking, and continuous improvement drive our passion for innovation. We challenge the ordinary, put safety first, and embrace new ideas while delivering practical, cost\-effective solutions that move our business forward. We **own what we do** . From day\-to\-day tasks to major projects, we take pride in our work and responsibility for the outcome. We don’t walk past problems we address them together. Our expertise, reliability, and care reflect who we are and the difference we make. And above all, we act with **trust and integrity** . Trust is built through open communication, honesty, and accountability. It allows us to move quickly, make confident decisions, and deliver results — knowing our people are empowered to do the right thing. Our journey depends on talented, committed people who want to make an impact. **Take the first step and join us on the journey.** *Panda is committed to attracting and retaining a skilled and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate and the people we serve. (DE\&I Policy Statement)* Apply Share * Share Vacancy on Facebook * Share Vacancy on X * Share Vacancy on LinkedIn * Share Vacancy on WhatsApp * Send Vacancy to a friend

title: Financial Accountant - Start up
description: A rapidly growing healthcare group is seeking to appoint a hands\-on Financial Accountant to join its finance team ahead of the launch of a new specialist centre in Dublin City. This is a rare opportunity to join a highly ambitious healthcare venture at an early stage and play a key role in helping build and shape the finance function from the ground up. The wider group also includes an established dermatology business and a growing direct\-to\-consumer e\-commerce brand, offering excellent exposure across multiple entities and revenue streams. Working closely with an experienced Finance Director, this role will suit someone who enjoys variety, thrives in a fast\-paced environment, and wants strong exposure across bookkeeping, management accounts, process improvement, and operational finance. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist with the preparation of monthly management accounts across multiple entities * Post month\-end journals including accruals, prepayments, payroll and intercompany entries * Prepare balance sheet reconciliations and control account reviews * Assist with financial analysis and ongoing process improvements * Work closely with senior leadership on finance projects and operational improvements * Support and mentor junior finance team members as the function grows * Build strong relationships across the wider business **Experience:** * Qualified Accountant ACA, ACCA, CIMA or CPA * Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills * Strong communicator with the ability to work closely with non\-finance stakeholders **Strong base salary and package attached.** **How to apply:** Contact Sinéad Canning on 086 800 6785 for more information about this position. You can also apply below with your CV. **About Engage People** Engage People is a specialist recruitment firm in Dublin, Ireland that connects clients and candidates in the Accountancy \& Financial and Banking \& International Financial Services sectors.

title: Financial Controller
description: **Financial Controller** **Dublin** Armont are partnering with a leading Dublin‑based PLC to appoint a Financial Controller on an initial 6‑month contract. Reporting at director level, you will lead an experienced finance team and oversee financial reporting across both established and newly formed entities within growing regions. The role will suit a qualified ACA or ACCA with finance management experience in a PLC, multinational or shared‑service environment. It is also a strong fit for a Big 4 trained ACA at senior manager or associate director level seeking a first move into industry. This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious financial controller or senior finance manager looking for a new challenge. This business has demonstrated a number of examples of contract roles being extended or converted to permanency. **The Role** * Lead and support the finance team in day‑to‑day operations * Oversee balance sheet management, account reconciliations, and financial reporting * Partner with finance leads to define responsibilities and ownership across shared accounting areas * Review monthly P\&Ls and deliver analysis for quarterly finance discussions * Ensure compliance across sales entities and coordinate timely filing of required returns * Review account reconciliations and provide clear commentary * Work with auditors to fulfil reporting and audit obligations * Provide supporting data for quarterly and annual reporting cycles * Drive process enhancements and improve team efficiency * Contribute to ad hoc finance initiatives as needed **The Candidate** * Qualified finance manager – minimum 3 \+ years’ finance management experience in a multinational environment * Alternatively Big 4 trained ACA at senior manager or associate director \+ level * ERP knowledge required * Strong leadership qualities and experience of team development **The Rewards** * Salary range, to be discussed (DOE) * Bonus * Pension * Healthcare * Flexible working * Hybrid working * Parking

title: Foundry R&D Software Engineer II
description: **Our Purpose** *Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.* **Title and Summary** Foundry R\&D Software Engineer II Who is Mastercard? Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all. Overview Mastercard is looking for a talented Software Engineer to join the Mastercard Foundry Research and Development team in our Dublin Tech Hub. In this role you will be part of a highly agile team building exiting and innovative products delivered at scale to global markets. Our team is built on a foundation of research and development, mining innovation internally, innovating new product lines with emerging technology, managing new products from inception to market validation and engaging strategically with start\-ups to shape the future of commerce with and for our customers. At Mastercard you will help define the future of commerce globally. This team will have a diverse focus both in terms of geography and variety of technology challenges driving hard to bring innovative payment solutions to market. Role This role involves working with a team of talented developers to develop high performance Java based solutions. In this role, you will be responsible for: * Design and implementation of innovative capabilities using AI Tooling. * Work as part of an agile team. * Work closely with technical leads and other developers to define solutions. * Research alternative technical solutions to meet changing business needs. * Produce design and research documentation. * Transitioning R\&D solutions to Mastercard core. Skills * Proficiency with Java JDK 17 or greater. * Knowledge of Tomcat, Spring, Spring Boot, Shell Scripting, JSON. * Experience with Docker, Kubernetes or other container orchestration solutions. * Expertise in Junit or other automated unit testing frameworks. * Experience with a UI framework such as React. * Knowledge of CI/CD principles and tools such as Jenkins, Gradle, Maven. * Passionate about software development and comfortable in a fluid environment. * Knowledge of Source Control Management and working with code in a multi\-developer environment. * Knowledge of or passion for mobile application development would also be an advantage * Demonstrates hands\-on knowledge of agents, skills, MCP integration, and practical AI workflow concepts. Other * Excellent communication (verbal and written) and strong collaboration skills. * Show initiative and demonstrate a willingness to take on challenging opportunities. * Passionate about code quality and coding best practices. * Highly motivated and a strong team\-player. **Corporate Security Responsibility** All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: * Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; * Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; * Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and * Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: From Sales to Recruitment – Where Performance Gets You Promoted
description: **From Sales to Recruitment – Where Performance Gets You Promoted** * **Stop waiting for your turn and start progressing through results** * **Join a company where honest feedback helps you grow faster** * **If you’re ambitious, this is where your career finally catches up to your potential** We’re **Access** , one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment companies. Progression here is built around performance, accountability, and people willing to push themselves further. Insurance sales have already prepared you for pressure, targets, and resilience. As a **Recruitment Executive** , you’ll take those skills into a faster\-moving environment where your progression, earnings, and responsibility can accelerate significantly. We’ve seen people move from sales environments into recruitment and quickly take on bigger responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and stronger earning potential than they expected possible. **What’s in it for you?** * **Merit\-based promotions** — meaning your progression is determined by performance, not tenure * **Honest coaching and mentorship** — helping you improve faster and develop real confidence * **Leadership opportunities early in your career** — allowing you to influence and mentor others sooner * **Continuous training and development** — helping you evolve into a high\-performing recruitment professional * **Financial growth that scales with your success** — in your first 2 years, you can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ **What we’re looking for:** * Has 1 to 3 years of experience in Insurance Sales, Outbound Sales, Broker Sales, Financial Services, Account Management, or similar * Comfortable in performance\-driven environments * Resilient, ambitious, and motivated by progression * Strong communication and relationship\-building skills **Additional Information:** Full\-time, office\-based role in Dublin City Centre, Monday to Friday. In your first 2 years, you can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ OTE, with accelerated progression based on performance. For more information, contact Ines Duarte confidentially at \+353 1 572 118 1 or email ines.duarte@accesshealthcare.ie

title: Full Stack Developer
description: **Virtu** Virtu is a leading financial firm that leverages cutting edge technology to deliver liquidity to the global markets and innovative, transparent trading solutions to our clients. As a market maker, Virtu provides deep liquidity that helps to create more efficient markets around the world. Our market structure expertise, broad diversification, and execution technology enables us to provide competitive bids and offers in over 19,000 securities, at over 235 venues, in 36 countries worldwide **THE ROLE** We are looking for an exceptional candidate to join our development team. Our team is a talented group of versatile software engineers who collaborate with the entire Virtu group to build the next generation of web\-based trading tools for our fast paced firm. Members of this team collaborate closely with traders, operations, and other developers to gather and understand needs, and implement real\-time trading, visualization, analysis and reporting tools. Ideal candidates will have a passion and talent for designing great, intuitive user interfaces as well as scalable back\-end services. **THE CANDIDATE** * 2\+ years of experience * Expertise with web client languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript) * Fluency with web client frameworks (React, Node), remote API design and architecture (REST, WebSockets) * Experience with source code management (Git is a plus) * Experience with databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, or KDB * Object oriented design skills and/or familiarity with Java a plus * Python experience is a plus * Interest in trading and financial market This role is based in our Dublin office with some flexibility to work hybrid. **THE PROCESS** Following an initial resume screen, candidates will be invited to complete an online programming test. Those who perform well will be contacted to arrange a phone screen. *Virtu Financial is an equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, welcoming you for who you are and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.*

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: **Hiring: Full Stack Engineer (Agentic AI)** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Flexible Hybrid Model) Are you a developer who is obsessed with the transition from traditional LLMs to **Autonomous Agentic Systems** ? We are currently representing a **leading\-edge Digital Innovation Hub** based in Dublin. They are scaling a specialized team focused on building enterprise\-grade, multi\-agent orchestrations and decision\-making systems. This isn't just about API integration; it’s about pioneering the next wave of AI design patterns. **The Role at a Glance** * **Tech Stack:** Python (FastAPI/Django), React.js, LangChain, OpenAI Agents. * **Focus:** Engineering "Agentic AI" solutions that move beyond chatbots into true autonomous orchestration. **What You’ll Be Doing** As a Full Stack Engineer, you will bridge the gap between robust software engineering and cutting\-edge AI: * **Architect \& Build:** Design full\-stack applications with a Python backend and React frontend. * **AI Orchestration:** Develop multi\-agent systems and decision\-making frameworks. * **Lead Innovation:** Evaluate the latest Agentic AI design patterns and drive their adoption within the enterprise. * **Observability:** Implement tracking for AI performance, evaluation, and reliability. * **Agile Delivery:** Work in a high\-octane DevOps environment to deliver production\-ready code in iterative sprints. **What We’re Looking For** * **Experience:** 3–5 years in full\-stack application development. * **Python Mastery:** Deep proficiency in Python and modern frameworks (FastAPI, Django, or Flask). * **Frontend Expertise:** Advanced React.js skills (Hooks, State Management, TypeScript). * **The AI Edge:** Hands\-on experience with **LangChain, OpenAI Agents** , or similar agentic frameworks. * **Cloud \& Scale:** Proven experience with **AWS, Azure, or GCP** and containerization ( **Docker/Kubernetes** ). * **Education:** A degree in Engineering or Computer Science (Master’s preferred). **Why Join This Team?** This is a rare opportunity to move past the "hype" of AI and into the **implementation** phase. You will be encouraged to explore new AI tools to optimize the SDLC and work with a global team of high\-performing engineers.

title: Full Stack Engineer, Services Technology
description: **Summary** Join IS\&T's Services Technology team and build cutting\-edge IT systems for Apple TV Studio \& Production and Music operations. As a Full Stack Engineer, you'll architect and develop large\-scale web applications, collaborating with cross\-functional teams to deliver high\-quality solutions in a fast\-paced environment.
