title: Full Time Retail Sales Consultant- Tallaght
description: Role Title: Retail Sales Consultant – Full Time –Tallaght Business Area: Retail Store Tallaght About This Role: This is more than just a retail job—it’s your chance to build a career in an exciting and fast\-paced industry. As a **Retail Sales Consultant** , you’ll: * Deliver outstanding customer service. * Work in a target\-driven environment that rewards achievement. * Be part of a team where your input is valued and your success is celebrated. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a busy setting and enjoys sales and working with people. You'll receive full training, so even if you’re new to telecoms, you’ll be set up for success. ****Benefits Include**** * Earn your annual salary of €28,798 \+ Attractive commission structure! * Continuous support, learning and opportunities for advancement. * Enhanced earning potential through various staff incentives. * Staff offer. * Free eir Wellness Programme \& Employee Assistance Programme. * €500 Employee Referral Scheme. * Discounted healthcare plan. * Pension scheme contribution. * 22 days annual leave. * Generous family friendly policies, including fertility, pregnancy loss, menopause and others. * Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion. * Paid leave benefits incl. maternity and sick leave benefits. Tax saver tickets and bike to work scheme. Why This Role: As a **Retail Sales Consultant** , your key responsibilities will include: * Customer Engagement: Build rapport with customers, understand their needs, and recommend the most suitable mobile and broadband solutions. * Sales Delivery: Consistently meet or exceed individual sales targets while contributing to the store’s overall performance. * Customer Experience: Handle queries and issues efficiently, ensuring every customer leaves satisfied. * Store Operations: Support daily administrative tasks, including stock control, data entry, and cash handling. * Product \& Policy Knowledge: Stay up\-to\-date with eir’s product offerings, promotions, and store policies to ensure accurate and compliant service delivery. * Market Awareness: Keep informed of competitor offerings so you can provide customers with the most competitive and relevant information. Flexibility is essential—you’ll be required to work evenings and weekends in line with store trading hours. Expectations From The Role: ****Knowledge, Skills And Abilities**** * Confidence in engaging with a variety of customers and understanding their needs. * Clear, effective communication skills with a strong ability to explain products and services. * A positive, target\-driven mindset with a passion for providing great service. * Interest in mobile phones, broadband, and emerging technologies. ****Qualifications**** * 1–2 years of experience in retail or sales is desirable, but relevant life experience will also be considered. * A strong customer focus and a proactive attitude are key to success in this role. Requirements For A Successful Application: **Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities** * Candidates must be able to confidently engage our customers * Candidates must be able to communicate simply and clearly in a manner that invokes trust * Candidates must have an interest in mobile phones **Qualifications** * 0\- 2 years retail or sales experience is desirable, although relevant life experience will also be considered. Others: At eir, we’re all about building connections—whether that’s through our products or our people. Working here means being part of a team that’s passionate about customer experience and driven by results. If you're looking for a role where you can grow your career, develop your leadership skills, and make a real impact—this is the place for you. eir reserves the right to conduct appropriate suitability checks in relation to prospective employees including but not limited to reference checking and/or other searches using publicly available information. We are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, and we will work with you to meet your needs. **\#eirforall.**

title: Full-Stack Developer
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin or Galway.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. We're looking for a permanent, full\-time Full\-Stack Developer to join our Software team to work on real\-world, high\-impact engineering and sustainability problems. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an experienced Full\-Stack Developer to work on multiple active projects in a client\-facing engineering team. **As a Full\-Stack Developer At Arup, You Will** * Design, build, and maintain web applications (both server\-side\-rendering and SPA) * Collaborate with team members and stakeholders to deliver project requirements * Implement automation scripts and integrations where needed * Contribute to architecture and technical decisions * Work across multiple projects and adapt to new technologies **Does this role inspire you?** **These Are Essential Skills And Qualifications For The Position** * Production experience with React.JS or Angular * Strong Node.js backend experience * Working knowledge of SQL and relational databases * Experience with serverless (AWS Lambda or Azure Functions) * Strong communication and collaboration skills for direct interaction with business stakeholders * Comfortable switching between multiple projects and learning new tools/technologies **We Also Value** * Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS/Azure), CI/CD, devOps tooling * Experience writing automation tools/scripts in Python * Exposure to microservices or distributed systems * Experience with express\+ejs **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Full-Time Experienced Baker / Pastry Chef
description: **Croissantly Bakery – Dún Laoghaire, Dublin** **Full\-Time \| Early Morning Shifts \| Immediate Start** Croissantly Bakery is growing, and we’re looking for an **experienced, passionate baker** to join our team. This is a hands\-on role for someone who genuinely loves artisan baking and can work confidently in a fast\-paced bakery environment. We are looking for someone skilled in **viennoiserie, dough lamination, bread production, and pastry preparation**, with the ability to work independently while also helping lead the team. **What you'll be doing:** * Producing high\-quality croissants, viennoiserie, breads, pastries, buns, and seasonal specials * Dough mixing, fermentation management, shaping, proofing, baking, and finishing * Advanced lamination work and pastry production * Managing daily production schedules efficiently * Ordering ingredients and helping with stock control * Supporting and guiding junior team members * Bringing fresh ideas and helping develop new products * Maintaining excellent hygiene and food safety standards * Ensuring consistency, quality, and attention to detail across all products **What autonomously with minimal supervision ✔ Leadership mindset — able to manage staff and production flow ✔ Creative and innovative with product development ideas ✔ Passionate, reliable, and highly motivated ✔ Comfortable with very early starts (typically 4 AM / 5 AM starts) ✔ Strong organisational and time management skills ✔ Team player with a positive attitude ✔ Full driving licence preferred **Bonus if you have:** * Experience with commercial bakery equipment * Experience in artisan sourdough production * Pastry finishing / dessert skills * Previous team leadership experience **What we €27,000\.00\-€38,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: GSC Market Logistics Specialist
description: **Role Summary** The GSC Market Logistics individual contributor is operationally accountable for Market Logistics activities in the Irish market leading and directing team members in each of those. The incumbent coordinates material movement between dock, warehouse and first paying customer locations with the goal of creating superior customer experience in a financially viable operation in pursuit of our purpose. The GSC Market Logistics role performs activities in planning \& controlling the efficient flow of product, materials, or services from the point of in\-country arrival through supply chain endpoint, meeting customer requirements. Accountable that product arrives within acceptable parameters, ensuring that time objectives are achieved within existing financial constraints to meet associated targets and goals of both GSC and business partners. Accountable for the execution and continuous improvement of standard Market Logistics processes \& systems, building relationships with strategic customers and partners through Market Logistics initiatives in support of vision, purpose, and goals. **Logistics Operation** * Coordinate with various teams to ensure efficient scheduling, procurement, and movement of finished products and samples, managing storage and delivery to customer locations. * Oversee import and export compliance, process free trade agreements accurately, and maximize financial returns within local and global constraints. **Transportation and Distribution:** * Formulate and implement strategies for efficient goods distribution across channels, including e\-commerce and wholesale. * Manage transportation by selecting carriers, optimizing routes, and overseeing freight costs for timely deliveries. * Work with logistics partners to ensure smooth operations and adherence to regulations, and coordinate with internal teams for compliant shipments. * Regularly evaluate trade channel performance, adjust for growth and customer satisfaction, and ensure timely compliance with serialization mandates. **LSP (Logistics Service Provider) Management:** * Collaborate with and manage LSPs, including freight forwarders, 3PLs, and carriers, to align with business needs. * Evaluate and select LSPs based on capability, performance, and cost, and negotiate contracts to meet business and performance goals. + Conduct regular performance reviews, business review, and audits, manage shipping activities and invoices, and ensure compliance with Environment Health \& Safety, Compliance, GDP, GMP, and any other legal standards. + Ensure On\-Time Ship, On\-Time Delivery (manage obstacles), Costs are in line with budget, Continuous Improvement programs. + LSP responsibilities for business continuity, controlled substances, dangerous goods \& Environment Health \& Safety; supported by Pfizer subject matter experts. + Support the Logistics \& Supply Market Leader for all the “mission critical” areas of market transformation for a robust performance of a logistics network. **Logistics Budget Management:** * Manage the logistics budget to align with business goals, control costs, and identify savings. * Collaborate with finance and other teams to forecast logistics expenses and monitor profitability KPIs. * Calculate total cost of ownership for products, considering all logistics expenses. * Implement cost control measures and optimize supply chain profitability, balancing savings with customer satisfaction and quality. **Inventory Management:** * Preform daily inventory reconciliation between LSP and market systems. * Develop inventory strategies to optimize stock levels, minimize costs, and prevent stockouts. * Work with demand planners and procurement for accurate forecasting and efficient replenishment. * Collaborate with Demand Planning and Market Planning Hub for effective inbound supply processing. * Manage inventory removal and material destruction in compliance with local regulations and Pfizer policy. **Optimization of Storage:** * Analyze and enhance storage and warehouse operations by adopting lean principles and continuous improvement initiatives for optimal space use, inventory accuracy, and efficiency. * Regularly assess storage capacity and layout, reconfigure for improved space use, and adopt automation technologies. * Implement inventory management strategies like ABC analysis for efficient item placement and handling. * Use WMS and tracking technologies for precise inventory monitoring, and collaborate with teams for aligned forecasting and minimized excess inventory. **Customer Relationship Management:** * Develop CRM strategies to foster strong customer relationships, using CRM systems to analyze data for personalized communication and targeted marketing. * Segment customers to tailor offerings, engage proactively through various channels to resolve issues and gather feedback. * Implement loyalty programs to encourage repeat business and collaborate with sales and marketing for aligned customer strategies. * Monitor customer satisfaction and loyalty metrics like Net Promoter Score (NPS) to drive improvements and customer\-centric initiatives. **Asset Management** * Oversee inventory management, ensuring compliance with regulations, conducting audits, and managing write\-offs and destruction processes. * Optimize electronic asset management through data management in systems like SAP ECC, WMS, IBP, SHARK/PfISH, and SupplyIQ. * Trade Management **Financial** * Responsible for developing and achieving operating budgets, implementing cost improvement projects, and maintaining efficiency in Market Logistics operations. * Deliver project objectives on time using structured project management for various initiatives, including capability projects, product launches, and mergers. * Conduct activity/cost analysis, partner with vendors and PGS teams to drive total cost improvements. **Sustainability in Logistics:** * Implement sustainability initiatives in logistics, focusing on eco\-friendly transportation, packaging and energy efficiency. * Develop sustainable strategies with teams to optimize routes and use renewable energy, aiming to reduce carbon emissions. * Track and improve key sustainability metrics like carbon footprint, waste reduction, and energy consumption. * Support the implementation and maintenance of the calculation tools and software used to measure in\-market emissions from Transportation and Distribution. * Stay updated on sustainability trends and regulations, and promote environmental responsibility in the supply chain. **Operational Excellence** * Maintain and refresh standard work processes using the Atlas framework as PCT Lead, and systematically enhance process capabilities and organizational design with the GSC IMEx Eco System framework. * Apply an Operational Excellence (IMEx) mindset, tools, and concepts to improve capabilities and cost efficiency in partnership with logistics service providers, forwarders, brokers, and internal business/process partners. **Value beyond supply** * Encourage a culture of innovation and continuous improvement, forming cross\-functional teams to explore new technologies and ideas. * Stay informed about industry trends and emerging technologies to identify opportunities for innovation and maintain a competitive edge. Preferably in the fields of Supply Chain, Logistics Management, Industrial Engineering or alike * Role/Market Specific: 4 \+ years of relevant experience in Supply Chain, Planning * Experience leading business review with external partners * Experience leading cross\-functional team projects * Demonstrated business acumen, industry, and product knowledge; including trends, competitive landscape and industry best practices which can impact the business * Operational Excellence experience * Experience in Pharma industry considered an asset * CPIM or CSCP certification by APICS is preferred * Project Management **Soft Skills** * Strong communication and presentation skills, ability to interact with cross\-functional teams and external stakeholders * Ability to interpret/ analyze data, draw meaningful conclusions and influence key stakeholders by presenting comprehensive solutions and recommendations. * Detail\-oriented with strong organizational and problem\-solving abilities * Ability to multitask in a fast\-paced environment **System and digital** * ERP system – SAP and Kinaxis * Ability to use digital dashboards for data analysis (Tableau, BI, IQVIA,.) title: GTM Data & Performance Analyst
description: Created in 2003, Smartbox is the French and European number 1 in experience gifts, with 6\.5 million boxes sold each year, around €350 million annual turnover, in a gift box market, offering the widest range of experience gifts. For more than 20 years, we have been developing thousands of experiences to offer and an equal number of unforgettable moments to live. From the most iconic to the most unique, Smartbox's mission has always been to enable everyone to gift their loved ones the opportunity to live unique experiences covering the themes of stay, adventures, well\-being and gastronomy. In the short future, we want to be the point of reference when it comes to inspire people to live experiences. We are at a tipping point in terms of innovation on our market. The group employs 800 collaborators based across Europe. Smartbox gift boxes are sold in 10 European countries: France, Italy, Spain, Belgium, Switzerland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, Portugal and Germany. Pioneer in the sector and number 1 in most of these countries, significantly ahead of its competitors, the group relies on a network of approximately 150 partner retailers throughout Europe, i.e. 18,000 major points of, and distributes its products as well as to the websites of the various brands of the group and with online distributors such as Amazon. One of the keys to the group's success is to have built a network of 30,000 partners in Europe. The Group has been owned since 2013 by Otium Capital, which has been able to support more than 50 start\-ups and innovative companies thanks to the profits generated by the Smartbox Group. More than just a box, Smartbox has been one of the biggest success story in the industry in the last 20 years. **About Your Role:** As a GTM Data \& Performance Analyst, you will support the GTM Strategy and Sales teams by producing reliable analyses, reports and dashboards that help monitor partner performance and commercial activity. This is an execution‑focused analytical role. You will work on clearly defined topics and priorities, contribute to recurring reporting and analyses, and help ensure that commercial teams have access to accurate and consistent data to support decision‑making. You will collaborate closely with GTM Strategy, RevOps and Sales, and interact regularly with Product, Tech and Finance to provide data inputs and insights. **Key Responsibilities** **Data Analysis \& Partner Performance:** * Produce recurring reports and analyses on partner performance across predefined dimensions such as partner tier, product category and geography. * Monitor key performance indicators (e.g. revenue contribution, availability, catalog quality, NPS) and highlight notable variations or data issues. * Prepare structured analyses to support reviews of partner performance, under the guidance of GTM Strategy. * Support quarterly and monthly business reviews by preparing data extracts, charts and summaries. * Track sales performance against targets for Account Managers and BDMs, and prepare standard reports for review by GTM leadership. **Pricing \& Commercial Analysis Support:** * Support pricing and margin analyses by preparing data inputs, simulations and comparison reports. * Assist in modelling the commercial impact of predefined pricing or incentive scenarios. * Help monitor pricing consistency across markets and categories and flag anomalies to relevant stakeholders. * Provide analytical support for new commercial initiatives (e.g. new offer types or partner models), based on clearly defined scopes. **Dashboards \& Reporting:** * Maintain and update existing GTM dashboards used by GTM Strategy, Sales teams and country stakeholders. * Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reports and dashboards. * Implement dashboard changes and updates based on documented business requirements. * Support the adoption of dashboards by commercial teams by helping explain metrics and reports. **Marketplace \& Product Data Support:** * Develop a good understanding of Smartbox’s marketplace and catalog performance metrics. * Track predefined catalog health indicators and prepare reports for Product and Operations teams. * Provide data and analysis to support cross‑functional discussions on marketplace performance when requested. * Assist in compiling competitor and market data to support GTM analyses. **Requirements for the Role:** * 2–4 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence or a related analytical role. * Good working knowledge of SQL and at least one BI or data visualisation tool (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Metabase, Power BI). * Basic understanding of commercial, sales or marketplace environments. * Ability to work with structured datasets and follow established analytical frameworks. * Comfortable collaborating with multiple teams (Sales, GTM Strategy, RevOps, Product, Finance). * Strong attention to detail and data quality. **Skills:** * Ability to execute data analyses accurately and on time. * Structured and methodical approach to problem solving. * Ability to summarise data and findings clearly. * Good communication skills, with the ability to explain metrics and reports to non‑technical stakeholders. * Willingness to learn and develop analytical and commercial skills. * Adaptability in a dynamic business environment. **What we value:** * **Reliability**: you deliver high‑quality analyses that teams can trust. * **Curiosity**: you are eager to understand the business and how data supports it. * **Collaboration**: you work effectively with different teams and functions. * **Growth mindset**: you are motivated to develop your skills and take on increasing responsibility over time.

title: General Operative
description: **Company:** Dawn Meats Kildare **Job Title:** General Operative. **Reporting To:** Production Manager. **Position type:** Permanent **Role Summary:** Dawn Meats Kildare have opportunities for General Operatives to join our production team. We are seeking reliable and hardworking General Operatives to join an industry leader that provides opportunities for training and progression to the right candidates. **Responsibilities** **The successful candidate will be based at our primary production facility in Kildare town and will be responsible for:** * Role based primarily in the Boning Hall; * Successful candidates will work as part of a team in the factory, packing meat cuts and trims to customer specification on a paced production line to fulfil production targets on a daily and weekly basis; * To assist on the production area \& provide support to the production team; * Work in all areas of the factory; * Keep good housekeeping standards in all areas; * Keep up to date knowledge of all products; * Keep up to date with all training to ensure you are trained to complete your day to day role; * Follow work instructions in all areas; * Ensure you comply with Health \& Safety regulations; * Ensure all data to be recorded is accurate and up to date; * Ensure quality guidelines are followed for all areas; * Undertake flexible working hours to meet deadline. **Qualifications** **Your role will see you tackle a wide range of business challenges in a busy production environment.** **The ideal Candidate will have or demonstrate:** * Entry level, semi\-skilled \& advanced positions available; * An ability to work to deadlines; * An ability to work on own initiative; * Interested in future development \& progression. INDLP

title: Geomatics Associate
description: At Dalcour Maclaren, we're key players in the Utilities and Infrastructure industry, bringing the UK and Ireland closer to Net Zero every day. Our land, environmental and geospatial experts work together to unlock the consents needed by our clients to deliver key projects. Our services include land access and assembly, agricultural liaison, planning, environmental impact assessments and geospatial mapping. Join us, and play your part in helping us to achieve our vision; to be the professional services team of choice, leading the Utilities and Infrastructure industry to a sustainable future. Due to continued and strong anticipated growth in our workstreams in the Republic of Ireland (RoI), the Geomatics team have an opportunity for an Associate to join our team in Dublin. The successful candidate will work closely with the GIS and Land Referencing Service Delivery Leads as well as the Land and Property team delivering on infrastructure projects across Ireland. The candidate will also support the Ireland Growth lead with business development activities including tendering and client engagement. The role will involve the management of all spatial and property diligence deliverables associated with the delivery of infrastructure projects at all stages from site/route appraisal through to construction. It will also include the provision of strategic advice to clients in relation to property diligence matters and the requirements associated. **The successful candidate will:** * **(Be required to work from our Dublin office a minimum of three days per week due to the leadership and management responsibilities associated with this role).** * Have experience of working with property records and information systems including Tailte Éireann along with associated knowledge of relevant infrastructure consenting regimes. * Have knowledge and experience of land rights and the methods by which they can be procured either through negotiation or through statutory means including CPOs (Ordú Ceannach Éigeantach) or Statutory Property Notices (Wayleaves/Easements). * Have practical GIS experience and ideally within the property and/or the utility/energy sector. * Have strong knowledge of ESRI ArcGIS software for spatial data capture, management and analysis. * Have project management experience including creation and management of project programmes, forecasts and budgets as well as the ability to provide reports detailing performance against agreed key performance indicators. * Be commercially aware. * Have great leadership qualities. * Be highly driven with strong communication skills. * Have a degree or master’s in GIS or a closely related subject such as Geography, Surveying, Land Management with a large GIS component. **As a Geomatics Associate you will:** * Be a member of the Geomatics Leadership team, working collaboratively with the wider Land Referencing and GIS teams to deliver an excellent innovative service and build strong client relationships. * To influence the direction of the Geomatics team in the Republic of Ireland and to deliver an excellent innovative service. * Build strong client relationships. * Report progress against Dalcour Maclaren’s Republic of Ireland business plan centring on people, service and sustainable growth, innovations, business development/opportunities, good news stories, lessons learnt and own and escalate risks. * Lead in identifying key data sources, engaging with suppliers to licence, obtain and set up the required environment and process to gain best value of them. * Manage projects and requests, ensuring requirements and objectives are met, building positive relationships and taking ownership of the standard of work. * Be able to accurately assess the time requirements of a request and be confident in advising clients and surveyors. * Fully understand all client requirements to provide guidance to junior team members and quality assure deliverables to ensure standards are met at all times. * Support the development of more junior members of staff by line managing, providing feedback and conducting their progress reviews. **Benefits:** * Annual bonus scheme * 25 days annual leave plus 1 day to celebrate your birthday * Vitality Health Membership (including Private healthcare, subsidised gym membership and other discounts) * Flexible working through our ‘Flexible Advantage’ supporting working families \& your work/life balance * Pension Scheme via The Peoples Pension * Life insurance (4x Base Salary) * Free on\-site parking in non\-city centre offices * Cycle to work scheme * 26 Weeks paid Maternity Leave * 4 Weeks paid Paternity Leave * BHN Extras – provides discounted gift cards for hundreds of popular retailers * Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice Scheme (provided by Octopus Electric Vehicles) effective at 1 years’ service * Sabbatical Policy effective at 3 years’ service * Professional development – paid study leave * Military service paid leave (7\.5 days per year) * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) plus wider Mental Health \& Wellbeing support * 2 x Company\-wide events every year, in addition to multiple office socials throughout the year *Dalcour Maclaren is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Global Network Analyst - Summer Intern
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** U.S. Bank’s Global Network Management (GNM) team provides centralized governance and oversight of the bank’s global sub‑custodian, cash correspondent, digital asset (crypto), and depositary networks, supporting U.S. Bank’s global custody, fund services, and securities servicing platform. Operating within Wealth, Corporate, Commercial and Institutional Banking (WCIB), the GNM team is responsible for network strategy, market access, due diligence, risk oversight, and ongoing performance management across domestic and international markets. Through close partnership with operations, risk, compliance, legal, tax, and product teams, Global Network Management helps ensure safe, resilient, and compliant market access, consistent service standards, and robust asset‑protection controls across traditional securities, cash, and digital asset ecosystems Our 12\-week paid summer internship program is one of the ways we help early talent thrive at Elavon U.S. Bank. Throughout the summer, you'll have the chance to explore different career pathways, contribute meaningfully to projects across the company and build lasting relationships with teammates, peers and leaders. **Key Responsibilities** * Market \& Industry Research: Research global custody, sub‑custodian banks, cash correspondent networks, and market infrastructure. Summarize key market developments, risks, and trends for the team. * Regulatory \& Market Practice Monitoring: Track regulatory and market practice changes impacting custody and cash networks (e.g., EU and global regulations) and support the preparation of concise impact summaries. * Data \& Reporting Support: Collect and organize data related to sub‑custodian oversight, due diligence, and network performance. Assist with management reports, dashboards, and presentations. * Sub‑Custodian Oversight Support: Assist network managers with due diligence activities, documentation tracking, meeting notes, and follow‑up actions as part of ongoing third‑party risk oversight. * Project \& Managerial Support: Support global network managers with day‑to‑day tasks and ad‑hoc projects related to network governance, market coverage, and process improvements. * Stakeholder Coordination: Help coordinate communication with internal partners (Operations, Risk, Compliance, Legal) and support preparation of materials for external network partners. Being fully immersed in our business will kick\-start your career journey as you learn more about yourself, our culture and the work we do. Our employees come from all kinds of personal and professional backgrounds and each person's career path is a unique journey. As an intern you'll receive: * Impactful, hands\-on experiences that keep you engaged and motivated all summer * Supportive community of peers * Social and volunteer engagements that build meaningful connections * Professional development, leadership exposure and mentoring * Pathway to full\-time offer through our development programs **Who We’re Looking For** We’re looking for intellectually curious and creative thinkers who can think on their feet and thoughtfully react to a changing market. **Core Bahaviours** * Collaboration – Build partnerships and promote a culture of teamwork. * Customer Centricity – Build strong customer relationships and deliver customer centric solutions. * Drive for Results – Hold self and others accountable to consistently achieve meaningful results. * Ethics \& Trust – Maintain high ethical standards; gain the confidence and trust of others through honesty, integrity and authenticity. * Inclusion – Build a culture of equity and inclusion in which diverse ideas, talent and perspectives are welcomed and encouraged. * Risk \- Appropriately identify and manage risks, in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, and with Company Policy. **Qualifications** * Education: Currently enrolled in an undergraduate program, preferably in Finance, Economics, or a related field. * Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data. * Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Attention to Detail: High level of accuracy and attention to detail in all tasks. * Proficiency in MS Office: Familiarity with Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel and PowerPoint. * Interest in Finance: A genuine interest in pursuing a career in finance, banking, specifically in custody network management. **Preferred Qualifications** * Solid understanding of financial and technology * Strong in Excel and PowerPoint **Location** * Cherrywood . The role offers a hybrid/flexible schedule, which means there's an in\-office expectation of 3 or more days per week and the flexibility to work outside the office location for the other days. **Contract Type:** * 12 week Fixed term Contract This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Graduate Boarding Assistant
description: Clongowes Wood College is one of Ireland’s oldest and most successful boarding schools with an enrolment of c.430\. The College is committed to the provision of the highest quality Catholic education in the Jesuit tradition. We are currently recruiting for a **Graduate Boarding Assistnat** A Graduate Boarding Assistant works alongside the Prefecting (Boarding ) team, including other recent graduates, in all aspects of their duties. Prefects in Clongowes are Housemasters or Housemistresses who are responsible for the day to day pastoral care of the students. The role will require a flexible approach to working but typical duties will include: * Help establish a home\-like, family atmosphere embodying the College’s Jesuit values, and facilitate possibilities for the boys to grow in their faith. * Supervising and leading students through their day to day routines. * Extracurricular activities, in particular sport, play an important part of life in Clongowes. The Prefecting team are involved in helping to coach sports teams. Similarly, Graduate Boarding Assistants are also expected to facilitate boys in their extracurricular and sporting interests. * Assist the Prefecting team with practical tasks in the dormitory and around the College. Duties include **two overnight shifts per week plus sporting commitments.** Graduate Prefects have accommodation within the College for the year, including full board, use of the College Library and sports facilities. The position will suit a graduate student who is considering a future in education and it is possible that the position can be combined with a teaching placement in Clongowes as part of a PME. The position would also suit someone who is seeking employment alongside other post\-graduate studies or has a passion for sport. The ideal candidate will have * An interest in working with young people, ideally within a teaching or coaching capacity. * Possess a high level of maturity with the ability to build strong relationships with boys, parents or colleagues. * Supportive of our Jesuit ethos. * Demonstrate relevant skills, experience and qualifications in an area of expertise, and genuinely enjoy sharing those skills for the development of young people. * Naturally possess a positive mind\-set. * Confident and persuasive written and verbal communicator. * Collaborative and flexible approach \& comfortable working as part of a team. * Calm under pressure; employ tact and diplomacy in difficult or sensitive situations. * Have a commitment to safeguarding and the welfare of young people. * Demonstrate a high level of emotional intelligence. * A good sense of humour and a sense of proportion. **Term** The post is fixed term from **late August 2026 to the end of June 2027\.** **Residency requirement** Successful candidates must be entitled to work and reside in Ireland. **Accommodation** Single accommodation onsite is included. **Safeguarding** The interview process will explore issues relating to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children. The appointment will be subject to the satisfactory outcome of Garda Vetting. Pay: €1,200\.00 per month Benefits: * Food allowance * Gym membership * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia)
description: We are seeking to recruit a Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia) to join our creative and technical team in producing engaging, interactive digital learning content for our extensive global client base. This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the design and development of high\-quality front\-end multimedia solutions that support over a million active corporate learners each year. The position is based in our Dublin office and provides an excellent foundation for a graduate looking to begin their career within a leading technology\-driven learning organisation. The ideal candidate will be a creative, curious, and motivated graduate with an understanding of front\-end and multimedia design principles and a passion for crafting visually compelling digital experiences. They should bring problem solving abilities, an eagerness to learn new tools and techniques, and a collaborative mindset. Attention to detail, a proactive approach, and effective communication skills will be essential in delivering polished, user\-centred solutions while continuously expanding their technical and creative capabilities. The successful candidate will work closely with an international team of instructional designers, software engineers, project managers, and client\-facing teams to deliver innovative multimedia content and interactive learning experiences. They will play a key role in transforming complex ideas into intuitive, engaging digital assets that support our clients’ goals in learning, performance improvement, and digital transformation worldwide. **This role involves:** •Transforming learning designs into interactive digital media optimised for phones, tablets, web browsers, and emerging AI\-enabled interfaces. •Using technologies such as Angular, Node.js, and Python as part this work to output intuitive user interfaces and user experiences in HTML, JavaScript, and CSS. •Influencing and advising cross\-functional teams, sharing ideas and contributing to collaborative project outcomes. •Creating high\-quality digital learning content using industry\-standard tools like Articulate Rise and Articulate Storyline. •Diagnosing, fixing, and troubleshooting issues across both traditional and AI\-enabled experiences to ensure smooth, reliable learner journeys. •Preparing and working with content and structured data for use with automation and AI systems such as Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. •Ensuring the accurate and effective implementation of digital media, maintaining consistently high standards of quality. •Testing and validating digital content across multiple platforms and Learning Management Systems, ensuring flawless performance. •Keeping up to date with fast\-evolving eLearning, multimedia, and online technology trends, bringing fresh ideas to the team. **Additional Skills \& Attributes:** •Curiosity and enthusiasm for new tools, technologies, modern development practices, conversational interfaces, AI\-assisted user experiences, and how design choices influence AI behaviour. •Willingness to continually learn and improve. •Strong attention to detail supported by an analytical mindset. •Effective communication skills and a collaborative working style. •Strong logical thinking and problem\-solving ability. •Appreciation of and interest in good design, both technically and visually. •Ability to think clearly about structure, consistency, and constraints when working with digital content. **Minimum requirements:** •Bachelor’s or master’s degree in a field related to multimedia, computer science, web development, or programming. •Exposure through academic or personal projects to data\-driven applications, conversational interfaces, or AI\-assisted tools is an advantage, but not required. •A good understanding of interface design, digital graphics or digital media, video, and audio. Intuition is an Equal Opportunities Employer

title: Graduate Health and Safety Advisors 2026
description: **Position:** Graduate Health and Safety Advisors 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroiand UK **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a Graduate Health and Safety Advisor, you will play a pivotal role in our project implementation and delivery processes. You will work closely with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to safely deliver the completion of projects. Your responsibilities will include managing and maintaining the company’s Safety Management System and CMS System. **Your Responsibilities will include:** * Providing general support and assistance to the JEG Site EHS Team * Assisting with site administration and weekly reports * Conducting weekly site audits * Assisting with Health and Safety Inductions * Maintaining training matrix and advise management of training requirements * Carrying out site inspections and audits with the EHS Team * Collecting and collating relevant information for inclusion in the Safety Management System **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Relevant 3rd Level Occupational Safety \& Health qualification essential * Proficiency in using Microsoft Office required * Excellent organisational and planning skills * Demonstrated ability to communicate and work well in a team environment **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note. We are currently not accepting application from agencies at this time.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Benelux Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in Dutch and English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate Sales and Application Engineer
description: **Launch Your Engineering Career with a Global Industry Leader** Are you a recent engineering graduate looking to combine technical problem\-solving with a future career in commercial engineering and sales? SPX Cooling Technologies is offering an exciting opportunity for a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer to join our Cooling Towers \& Accessories team in Ireland. This newly created role is designed to develop the next generation of technical and commercial talent within our business. You will gain hands\-on experience working on real HVAC and process cooling projects, learning how to review technical specifications, support quotations, and help deliver engineered solutions for customers across multiple industries. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys engineering detail, wants exposure to commercial projects, and is eager to develop toward a future customer\-facing engineering or sales role. **Why Join SPX Cooling Technologies?** * Join a global leader in cooling tower and thermal management solutions * Structured graduate development and mentoring from experienced engineers * Gain exposure to HVAC, process cooling, and industrial applications * Learn both the technical and commercial sides of engineering projects * Clear long\-term progression into applications engineering, technical sales, and leadership roles * Supportive team environment with ongoing training and development **The Role** As a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer, you will support the technical and commercial teams by assisting with product selection, proposal preparation, and engineering analysis. You will work closely with experienced colleagues to learn SPX systems, engineering tools, and customer requirements while developing your technical confidence and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Assist with reviewing RFQs, specifications, and technical drawings * Learn to use SPX selection software (QTC) to configure suitable solutions * Support the preparation of technical proposals and submittal documents * Help ensure product selections meet customer and project requirements * Work with internal engineering and sales teams on project coordination **Commercial \& Sales Exposure** * Support quotation and proposal development * Attend customer meetings and presentations alongside senior team members * Develop understanding of pricing, project delivery, and market competitors * Assist with maintaining project pipeline and sales data * Build commercial awareness within a technical engineering environment **What We’re Looking For** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Energy Engineering, Building Services Engineering, or similar * Strong interest in HVAC, cooling systems, thermodynamics, or industrial engineering * Good analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to interpret technical information and engineering drawings * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn * Motivated individual looking to develop a long\-term engineering career **Development \& Career Progression** This role has been designed as a long\-term development opportunity. You will receive structured support and mentoring with a clear pathway into: * Applications Engineering * Technical Sales * Key Account Management * Commercial Leadership If you are looking for a graduate engineering role that offers both technical depth and future commercial progression, we would love to hear from you. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€42,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Sales and Marketing Executive
description: Aline Office Furniture supplies new, used and rental office furniture solutions to customers across Ireland. From complete office fit outs to flexible rental options, we help our customers create professional and practical workspaces. We offer a fast\-paced and hands\-on environment where team members have the opportunity to take ownership and contribute ideas from day one. We are looking for an ambitious and creative individual to grow with the company. **The Role:** This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate to gain hands\-on experience in both sales and digital marketing within a fast\-paced commercial environment. The role will involve managing social media channels, supporting online advertising campaigns, analysing marketing performance, and assisting with sales and customer engagement. The successful candidate will play a key role in promoting our new, used, and rental office furniture offerings while helping to generate leads and grow brand awareness. **Key Responsibilities:** · Managing and scheduling content across social media channels. · Creating content such as product photos, short videos and promotional posts. · Keeping the website updated with product listings and completed office fit\-out projects. · Reviewing website and campaign analytics using Google Analytics. · Running Google Ads campaigns and monitoring ad performance. · Researching competitors, industry trends, and marketing ideas. · Following up on sales leads and customer enquiries by phone or email in a timely, professional, and effective manner. · Assisting with quotations and proposals for customers. · Maintaining customer records and updating CRM systems. · Supporting showroom presentation and product displays. · Coordinating with suppliers and internal sales staff. · Helping organise promotions, campaigns, and seasonal offers. · Assisting with email marketing campaigns and newsletters. · General administrative support for the sales and marketing team. **Candidate Profile:** · Recent graduate in Marketing, Business, Digital Media, Communications, or a related field. · Strong interest in sales, digital marketing, and business development. · Familiarity with social media platforms and content creation tools. · Excellent communication and organisational skills. · Creative, proactive, and commercially minded. · Strong attention to detail with the ability to multitask. · Ability to work independently and as part of a growing team. · Familiarity with Google Ads, Google Analytics, and digital marketing tools is preferred. **What We Offer:** · Competitive salary and benefits package. · 3pm finish on Fridays. · Hands\-on experience across sales and digital marketing from day one. · Training, mentorship, and support with professional development. · Supportive and collaborative team environment. · Opportunity to develop and own marketing campaigns and digital channels. Job Type: Full\-time, permanent Location: Dublin based, in person Pay: €31,000\.00\-€31,200\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Start Your Veterinary Nursing Journey the Right Way** ------------------------------------------------------- ***Anicare Veterinary Group – Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry*** ***Full\-time or Part\-time \\u007C Permanent*** ***€30,000 (SVN) – €32,000 (New Graduate RVN)*** --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ### **From Student to Skilled Nurse – With a Team Behind You Every Step of the Way** You’ve worked hard to get here \- late\-night study sessions, long placement days, and a genuine passion for animal care that’s carried you through. Now it’s time to take that next step. At Anicare Veterinary Group, we’re inviting final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses and newly qualified RVNs to join us before or just after graduation, so you can transition into practice with confidence, support, and structure already in place. This isn’t just your first job, it’s your launchpad. A place where you’re encouraged, guided, and given the space to grow into the nurse you’ve trained to become. ### **Learn By Doing – Build Confidence From Day One** We know how important those early months are in shaping your career. That’s why we’ve created an environment where learning happens naturally, every single day. You’ll be immersed in all aspects of small animal nursing within a busy, first\-opinion setting \- gaining hands\-on experience in surgical nursing, anaesthesia, inpatient care, and nurse consultations. But just as importantly, you’ll never be expected to figure things out alone. With structured onboarding, approachable mentors, and an experienced team that genuinely wants you to succeed, you’ll be supported to develop at your own pace. Ask questions, try new skills, and gradually take on more responsibility as your confidence grows \- exactly as it should. ### **Three Clinics, One Shared Purpose: Supporting You** Wherever you join us \- Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry \- you’ll find a slightly different environment, but the same supportive culture across the board. In **Blanchardstown**, you’ll work in a newly refurbished clinic with excellent facilities, including a dedicated dental suite, separate operating theatre, and DR x\-ray system. With certificate holders in feline medicine and diagnostic imaging on\-site, it’s an ideal setting if you’re curious, ambitious, and keen to deepen your clinical knowledge early in your career. In **Clontarf,** you’ll become part of a long\-established, collaborative team that genuinely enjoys working together. It’s a great fit if you value camaraderie, shared learning, and being surrounded by experienced colleagues who are invested in your development. In **Santry**, you’ll join a close\-knit nursing team in a well\-equipped first\-opinion clinic. You’ll play an active role in daily practice life \- from assisting with surgeries and monitoring anaesthesia to engaging with clients and helping manage the clinical environment \- building confidence through real responsibility, with support always close at hand. Across all locations, you’ll also benefit from being part of the wider Anicare network \- giving you access to shared knowledge, mentorship, and opportunities across multiple clinics. ### **A Rota That Supports Your Life (Not Just Your Career)** Starting your career shouldn’t mean burning out. We’ve designed our rota with balance in mind, so you can focus on learning while still enjoying life outside of work. You’ll typically work a 4\-day week, with around 1\.5 weekends in 6, and no out\-of\-hours or overnight commitments. That means you can build your skills in a sustainable, supportive way \- without the pressure of exhausting schedules. We also understand that flexibility matters. Whether you’re looking for part\-time hours or a slightly different working pattern, we’re happy to explore options that work for you. ### **Who Will Thrive in This Role** This opportunity is perfect for final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses graduating this summer or newly qualified RVNs who are (or soon will be) eligible for VCI registration. More than anything, we’re looking for attitude and enthusiasm. If you’re motivated to learn, enjoy being part of a team, and genuinely care about delivering great patient care, you’ll fit right in. You don’t need to know everything \- that’s what we’re here for. ### **More Than Your First Job – A Career That Grows With You** When you join Anicare, you’re not just stepping into a role, you’re joining a pathway. You’ll have access to structured development programmes, including our Graduate Nurse Academy, alongside additional in\-house training and learning opportunities designed to build both your clinical skills and your confidence. As you grow, so do your opportunities. Whether you discover a passion for surgery, inpatient care, client education, or leadership, we’ll support you in exploring and developing your interests over time. ### ### **A Team That Feels Like Home** Starting somewhere new can feel daunting, but you won’t feel like “the new person” for long. We pride ourselves on creating friendly, inclusive teams where everyone supports each other and celebrates progress together. From your very first day, you’ll be surrounded by people who remember what it felt like to be in your shoes and are genuinely invested in helping you succeed. ### **Supporting You, Professionally and Personally** We believe the best nurses are those who feel supported, valued, and able to grow. Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll benefit from a package designed to support your wellbeing and development. ### **Comprehensive Benefits Package – subject to pro rata** In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: #### **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave #### **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave \& Company sick pay * Free flu jabs #### **Development \& Career Growth** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities #### **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid #### **Additional benefits** * Pawesome benefits – various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) ### **Ready to Take That First Step?** If you’re about to graduate and looking for a place where you’ll be supported, challenged, and encouraged to grow into the best version of yourself as a veterinary nurse, we’d love to welcome you. Join Anicare as a student nurse now and step confidently into your future as a skilled, capable RVN. €32,000 per annum We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Graduate Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Start Your Veterinary Nursing Journey the Right Way** ***Anicare Veterinary Group \- Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry* *Full\-time or Part\-time \| Permanent* *€30,000 (SVN) \- €32,000 (New Graduate RVN)*** **From Student to Skilled Nurse \- With a Team Behind You Every Step of the Way** You’ve worked hard to get here \- late\-night study sessions, long placement days, and a genuine passion for animal care that’s carried you through. Now it’s time to take that next step. At Anicare Veterinary Group, we’re inviting final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses and newly qualified RVNs to join us before or just after graduation, so you can transition into practice with confidence, support, and structure already in place. This isn’t just your first job, it’s your launchpad. A place where you’re encouraged, guided, and given the space to grow into the nurse you’ve trained to become. **Learn By Doing \- Build Confidence From Day One** We know how important those early months are in shaping your career. That’s why we’ve created an environment where learning happens naturally, every single day. You’ll be immersed in all aspects of small animal nursing within a busy, first\-opinion setting \- gaining hands\-on experience in surgical nursing, anaesthesia, inpatient care, and nurse consultations. But just as importantly, you’ll never be expected to figure things out alone. With structured onboarding, approachable mentors, and an experienced team that genuinely wants you to succeed, you’ll be supported to develop at your own pace. Ask questions, try new skills, and gradually take on more responsibility as your confidence grows \- exactly as it should. **Three Clinics, One Shared Purpose: Supporting You** Wherever you join us \- Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry \- you’ll find a slightly different environment, but the same supportive culture across the board. In **Blanchardstown** , you’ll work in a newly refurbished clinic with excellent facilities, including a dedicated dental suite, separate operating theatre, and DR x\-ray system. With certificate holders in feline medicine and diagnostic imaging on\-site, it’s an ideal setting if you’re curious, ambitious, and keen to deepen your clinical knowledge early in your career. In **Clontarf,** you’ll become part of a long\-established, collaborative team that genuinely enjoys working together. It’s a great fit if you value camaraderie, shared learning, and being surrounded by experienced colleagues who are invested in your development. In **Santry** , you’ll join a close\-knit nursing team in a well\-equipped first\-opinion clinic. You’ll play an active role in daily practice life \- from assisting with surgeries and monitoring anaesthesia to engaging with clients and helping manage the clinical environment \- building confidence through real responsibility, with support always close at hand. Across all locations, you’ll also benefit from being part of the wider Anicare network \- giving you access to shared knowledge, mentorship, and opportunities across multiple clinics. **A Rota That Supports Your Life (Not Just Your Career)** Starting your career shouldn’t mean burning out. We’ve designed our rota with balance in mind, so you can focus on learning while still enjoying life outside of work. You’ll typically work a 4\-day week, with around 1\.5 weekends in 6, and no out\-of\-hours or overnight commitments. That means you can build your skills in a sustainable, supportive way \- without the pressure of exhausting schedules. We also understand that flexibility matters. Whether you’re looking for part\-time hours or a slightly different working pattern, we’re happy to explore options that work for you. **Who Will Thrive In This Role** This opportunity is perfect for final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses graduating this summer or newly qualified RVNs who are (or soon will be) eligible for VCI registration. More than anything, we’re looking for attitude and enthusiasm. If you’re motivated to learn, enjoy being part of a team, and genuinely care about delivering great patient care, you’ll fit right in. You don’t need to know everything \- that’s what we’re here for. **More Than Your First Job \- A Career That Grows With You** When you join Anicare, you’re not just stepping into a role, you’re joining a pathway. You’ll have access to structured development programmes, including our Graduate Nurse Academy, alongside additional in\-house training and learning opportunities designed to build both your clinical skills and your confidence. As you grow, so do your opportunities. Whether you discover a passion for surgery, inpatient care, client education, or leadership, we’ll support you in exploring and developing your interests over time. **A Team That Feels Like Home** Starting somewhere new can feel daunting, but you won’t feel like “the new person” for long. We pride ourselves on creating friendly, inclusive teams where everyone supports each other and celebrates progress together. From your very first day, you’ll be surrounded by people who remember what it felt like to be in your shoes and are genuinely invested in helping you succeed. **Supporting You, Professionally and Personally** We believe the best nurses are those who feel supported, valued, and able to grow. Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll benefit from a package designed to support your wellbeing and development. **Comprehensive Benefits Package \- Subject To Pro Rata** In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave \& Company sick pay * Free flu jabs **Development \& Career Growth** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Pawesome benefits \- various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) **Ready to Take That First Step?** If you’re about to graduate and looking for a place where you’ll be supported, challenged, and encouraged to grow into the best version of yourself as a veterinary nurse, we’d love to welcome you.
