title: Guest Services Host - Catering Assistant
description: We are seeking a dedicated candidate to assist our Rapport and Restaurant Associates teams in their daily operations. This role is designed to provide a supportive environment that fosters professional growth and skill development. The candidate will engage within various tasks including organizing workflow, communicating among the team members and enhancing overall efficiency of service delivery. Candidate should possess interpersonal skills, willingness to learn and genuine interest in helping others. This temporary role offers valuable experience in collaborative setting, promoting both personal and professional developments. You’ll be joining a collaborative EMEA team of 24 and continuing to expand. Despite being spread across the region, the team works closely together and is eager to welcome a new member. In this role, you’ll be a vital presence on\-site, fostering a positive, engaged workplace community, supporting employee satisfaction, and addressing any day\-to\-day challenges that arise. ***Type of contract:*** Part\-time, Fixed term ***Hours:*** 6 per week (Tuesdays \& Wednesdays: 3\-hours per day) **What will you get?** * **Financial Freedom:** Enjoy exclusive high\-street discounts and wellbeing benefits just one click away with Unlock, our benefits platform! * **Transportation Benefits:** We offer tax\-saver tickets on public transport in partnership with Travel Hub and/or Bike to Work Scheme (providing you with sustainable transport modes, incorporating physical activity into your everyday commuting). * **Secure Your Future:** Benefit from a contributory pension scheme using MyFutureFund, helping you save for retirement automatically. * **Health Matters:** In addition to our wellness programmes, we offer eye examination vouchers for those requiring a Visual Display Unit (VDU/DSE) test. * **Thrive Personally \& Professionally:** Unlock endless learning and development opportunities to elevate your career! * **Celebrate Excellence:** Shine a spotlight on outstanding peers with WOW Awards for their exceptional contributions. * **Give Back to Community:** Take one paid day off annually to support a cause you’re passionate about! **Main responsibilities** * Assisting with set up of client meeting rooms i.e. ensuring all note pads, pens etc. are always stocked. * Checking the rooms before and after each meeting. * Assisting with escorting visitors to client meeting rooms. * Assisting with preparations for events\- i.e. preparing and organizing name badges. * Assisting with Out of hours events\- escorting, guiding etc. * Assisting with Cloakroom management. * Supporting the Hospitality team in ensuring the refreshment area at the reception is well always kept neat and tidy. * Keeping the Grab \& Go fridge stocked up and replenishing any items needed in our Café(s) i.e. coffee cups, spoons etc. * Assisting with set up and clearance of Client meeting spaces pre and post meetings with food and beverage elements. **The ideal candidate will** * Share our Rapport values: ***Create Rapport, Be Brilliant, Innovative Spirit, Walk Tall and Stronger Together.*** * Ideally, have at least one year of experience in a similar role within a fast\-paced and dynamic establishment (e.g., five\-star hotel or corporate environment). * Be proactive, reliable, and flexible. * Continuously learn and provide support to immediate and the wider teams. * Adhere to company policies and procedures. * Look for opportunities for improvement and enhancement of our services. * Be effective communicator and team player. **About us** Rapport Guest Services is a multi\-award\-winning company, delivering front\- and back\-of\-house guest services for leading organisations across the UK, Ireland, and globally. As part of Compass Group UK\&I, we combine people\-first culture with exceptional service standards. We are proud of our commitment to inclusion, wellbeing, and creating opportunities for everyone to thrive. We are also a Disability Confident Employer, offering meaningful roles for individuals with additional needs. Find out more about us and our values at www.rapportservice.com. *Rapport job adverts always gain a high response rate, but we do review all applications. Please check your inbox and your junk/spam folder for our response to your application. Due to high application volumes, we recommend that you do not apply for more than one role at any time. Please ensure you apply for the role that best matches your experience.*

Whether you're looking for a long\-term career or just some extra shifts, if you're reliable, friendly and love working as part of a team \- this role could be perfect for you. This role is not suitable for under\-18s because of lone working. There may be other roles available, including front\-of\-house and kitchen roles. What's in it for you? * competitive rate of pay * a free meal and a drink, when working * 20% discount on all food, drinks and hotel accommodation (for you and up to three guests), when not working * €1 extra per hour, for hours worked during midnight\-5\.59am * bonus scheme \- earn up to 19% of your pay * availability of guaranteed\-hours contracts and variable\-hours contracts * paid holiday * free shares (after a qualifying period) * loyalty reward scheme The role At a glance, your role is to clean all areas of the hotel thoroughly, with the aim of enhancing the guests' experience by having excellent standards of cleanliness at all times. **Duties include:** * interacting politely with guests \- greeting them, saying 'thank you' and 'goodbye' * servicing bedrooms and all other areas of the hotel in line with our standard operating procedures * completing daily, weekly and monthly cleaning tasks, as required * following correct procedures when using cleaning chemicals and equipment * monitoring cleaning supplies and notifying the manager when more are needed * storing all laundry correctly and completing relevant documentation * adhering to stock\-control procedures and maintaining security of stock at all times * reporting any security issues, safety hazards, accidents, incidents and/or maintenance issues to the manager on duty * completing required induction, role\-specific and ongoing refresher training and contributing to continuous improvement Progression and development Most Wetherspoon team leaders and managers started as an associate. If you're looking to progress, we can support your development with: * structured on\-the\-job training. * apprenticeships and management academies. * cross\-training into other areas (bar, floor, kitchen). * opportunities to progress into team\-leader and management roles and beyond. Wetherspoon promotes an inclusive working environment where everyone is welcome. When you're at work, we want you to feel comfortable being yourself. Whatever your background, preferences or beliefs, we'll ensure that you have the skills and knowledge you need for a great career with us. If a high number of applications is received, this vacancy may close early. **HOURS PER WEEK:** 30

title: HubSpot Solutions Strategist
description: Salted Stone is a fast\-paced, full\-service digital marketing agency, and we are looking for a **HubSpot Solutions Strategist** to join our growing team. Our mission is to empower growth\-minded companies through creative solutions combined with smart technology. We believe nothing is impossible and always solve for the customer. We aim to attract, engage, and develop exceptional people and, in turn, deliver distinctive, lasting improvements for our customers. This is a mid\-level full\-time position reporting to the Director of Solutions on our Solutions Architecture team (commonly known as Technical Marketing elsewhere). The HubSpot Solutions Strategist will help our clients develop, implement, and optimize new technologies and processes that tie together the entire customer lifecycle using the HubSpot platform. The HubSpot Solutions Strategist supports the solutions team by cultivating professional expertise and applying this to address a range of technical marketing, sales, and service issues. They determine appropriate courses of action based on established guidelines and adapt processes and methods as necessary. Ideal candidates enjoy working directly with our clients and internal team to lead engagements from a technical standpoint (think: ideation up to execution), are organized, great communicators, and aren’t afraid to tackle challenges head\-on! **Job Responsibilities** * Complete and deliver all elements of the Salted Stones Solutions Team Product offerings (Data Migration, Portal Strategy, and HubSpot Onboarding including Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, etc.) * Interact directly with and provide a translation layer to technically\-oriented marketers and software developers, internally and on the client\-side. * Lead client\-facing calls, providing technical expertise and a complete understanding of tools to help clients understand and use offerings effectively. * Run solutions onboarding engagements (Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, and/or Service Hub) without relying on CS team members to run points/project management. * Work closely with the development team (both internally and externally) to connect existing systems to HubSpot. * Be able to generate a standard ERD for either existing or proposed integration structure. * Leverage understanding of data structures to provide recommendations and implementation of systems setup. * Be able to discuss 1st party, 3rd party, and Middleware integration solutions. * Troubleshoot integration and data problems. * Regularly thinks creatively on how to use the HubSpot Platform to solve for client needs. * Develop client\-facing supporting documentation and processes for new technology configurations, customizations, and functionalities. * Execute assigned tasks with a high degree of quality and on time. * Provide accurate time estimates for your work, work within timelines and budgets, and other applicable project management concerns. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the team by identifying areas for optimization, streamlining processes, and implementing innovative solutions. * Lead pre\-sales engagements from discovery through solution design, translating client business objectives into scalable, technically sound HubSpot strategies and clearly articulating value, approach, and scope to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Deliver tailored solution demonstrations that effectively articulate the value of HubSpot and proposed architectures, aligning platform capabilities to client\-specific requirements, use cases, and desired business outcomes. **Required Competencies** * + Successful experience in a Solutions Strategy, Technical Marketing, Marketing/Revenue Technologist, Marketing/Revenue Operations, etc., role (agency experience a plus); ideally suited for early to mid\-career level, but not entry level. + Proven experience with HubSpot (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub). + Excellent organizational skills and the ability to drive and participate in multiple client engagements at once. + Strong technical writing skills \& documentation skills, primarily for creation of compelling client\-facing deliverables. + Ability to confidently communicate with clients as needed. + Excellent project management, organizational and time management skills; and you're comfortable managing multiple projects (budgets, timelines, personalities) simultaneously. + Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Analytics, Databox, and similar analytics tools. + Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Tag Manager. + Basic knowledge of marketing and sales automation tools, business intelligence tools, data enrichment tools, etc. + Strong understanding of sales processes and CRM lifecycle management (pipeline design, lead lifecycle, attribution basics) Experience contributing to or supporting proposal/RFP responses and statements of work (SOWs). + Ability to lead structured discovery sessions, gathering and synthesizing business, technical, and data requirements. + Demonstrated consultative mindset, with the ability to challenge client assumptions and guide toward better solutions. **S2 General Requirements** * **Team player.**Friendly, authentic, collaborative, and makes an effort with relationships (the S2 team, clients, HubSpot). Shows care and respect for those around them and themselves. Live our Values. * **Growth mindset.** Prioritises learning and self\-improvement. Shows motivation and dedication to growth, personally and professionally. * **Agile**. Adapts to changing priorities and conditions, complexity, and change. * **Proactive**. Demonstrates tenacity and acts without being told what to do. Brings new ideas to S2\. Finds solutions and opportunities, not just problems. * **Prioritizes communication**\- Clear, transparent, and consistent with communication across your team and manager, with other teams, and with clients. If in doubt, over\-communicates. Isn’t afraid to call out and help correct mistakes before they impact the team or the business. * **HubSpot\-centric.** HubSpot is at the center of most work at Salted Stone. Not everyone has to be a HubSpot expert to work here, but everyone needs to have a baseline interest and understanding. **You’ll enjoy working:** In a hybrid role \- our team chose the balance of remote work and contact time in our Dublin office that best suits them and their roles. They’re split between Ireland and Perth, Australia so are used to doing things on Zoom and async. S2’s wider team is spread across North America, Australia, Ireland, and the Philippines so there may be times that we ask you to flex your schedule to allow for collaboration with different timezones. This flex goes both ways. **Benefits** Our Irish team enjoy: * Company paid health insurance and pension match * Birthday and Anniversary Leave (an additional paid day off) * Holisticly wellness benefit * Regular Culture Committee initiatives \& events * Cycle to Work Scheme * Flexible, Hybrid or Remote Work * Team Lunches \& Happy Hours * Company provided laptop **Application process** * Resume review * Initial call with the Recruitment Team * Full interview with the hiring Director \& Regional Director, EMEA * Practicum for select applicants * Reference checking

title: Human Resources Officer
description: The Irish Aviation Authority (IAA) is the single aviation regulator for Ireland. We are responsible for the regulation of safety, security and consumer interests. The IAA seeks a high\-achieving and motivated HR professional to join the Human Resources division of the IAA. A team of HR professionals reports to the Employee Relations Manager and are vital links to the HR division, the Chief People Officer, and the wider organisation. The primary portfolio for the successful candidate will be to ensure delivery of data\-driven activities, projects, and systems within the HR remit in support of our IAA, People and HR strategies. Other aspects of the role of HR Officer will be completing standard HR tasks and initiatives to ensure business continuity and all components of strategic HRM. This role offers the candidate an excellent opportunity for personal and career development. **Principal Duties and Responsibilities** As a member of the Human Resources team, you will have direct responsibility for the IAA’s HR duties including: * Deputise for the Employee Relations Manager as required * Support the Employee Relations Manager and Chief People Officer in strengthening the HR function and progressing HR Strategy in the IAA * Contribute positively to the development and embedding of IAA culture and core values * Contribute to the continuous improvement of HR technologies, processes, and data workflows to support efficient delivery of the HR Strategy **HR Data Analytics Tasks:** * Lead and support HR projects to ensure coordinated, timely delivery of data driven HR initiatives and system enhancements * Manage, validate, and maintain HR data used in organisational and people‑related projects * Prepare and coordinate HR data for project planning, tracking, audits, and reporting purposes * Produce and analyse regular and ad hoc HR reports, metrics, and dashboards to support projects and management decisions * Collaborate with project managers, finance, and other stakeholders to provide HR data insights and workforce information * Design, administer, and analyse HR surveys, and produce reports **Other HR Officer Tasks** * Manage and maintain accurate HR records and personnel files, ensuring high\-quality data is captured, stored, and used to inform business decision\-making * Participate in end\-to\-end recruitment \& selection campaigns including competition documentation, shortlisting, interviewing, contract development, and performing onboarding, induction and exit procedures for all staff members as required * Research, draft, and implement HR initiatives, policies, and procedures, and providing staff guidance and engagement * Support the IAA’s strategic HR programmes * Implement IAA compensation and benefit programmes and entitlements as required * Organise events, deliver staff communications, and update the staff intranet site * Source procurement contracts related to the HR function, and follow internal finance and procurement procedures including processing invoices, purchase orders, and budgets * Develop and deliver training to staff in key HR areas, including the use of HR systems, and HR procedures where relevant * Complete other Human Resources tasks and projects as required This job description is not exhaustive, other items may be added from time to time at the discretion of the Authority. The successful candidate should have the following qualifications and skills: **Required:** * Minimum 3 years’ relevant experience in an HR focused role delivering key HR projects, systems, and reporting * Certificate in Human Resources or equivalent accreditation, or demonstrable relevant work experience * Experience supporting core HR activities including recruitment and selection, learning and development, policy development, performance management, recognition, and associated administrative tasks * Demonstrable experience using and enhancing HR Information Systems (e.g. CoreHR/PeopleXD or similar) **HR Data Analytics:** * Experience managing, validating, and maintaining HR data used in organisational and people related projects * Experience preparing and coordinating HR data for project planning, tracking, audits, and reporting purposes * Experience producing and analysing regular and ad hoc HR reports, metrics, and dashboards to support projects and management decisions * Experience collaborating with multiple stakeholders to provide HR data insights and workforce information * Excellent IT skills and working knowledge of Office 365 and Microsoft Office Suite applications such as SharePoint, Teams, Word, PowerPoint, PowerBI and Outlook. * Solid working knowledge and understanding of Irish employment legislation with the ability to interpret clearly and succinctly * Experience drafting, formatting, editing, and proofreading documents (e.g., policies reports and procedures, staff communications), with a high standard of written and verbal English * Excellent organisational and planning skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and projects simultaneously * Strong problem\-solving and decision\-making skills * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with stakeholders at multiple levels both internally and externally, maintaining professionalism, integrity, and discretion * Excellent attention to detail * The ability to perform well both independently and within a strong team environment * Demonstrates a positive, flexible attitude, and willingness to learn new skills and systems to ensure delivery of best practice People Strategy * Displays key traits such as self\-confidence, creativity, initiative, and drive **Desirable** * Third\-level qualification in Human Resources or related field * Membership of professional HR networks (e.g. CIPD) * Strong Excel and PowerBI experience or other relevant analytics tools or platforms * Project management experience * Significant experience, interest, and/or proficiency involving data\-driven activities systems, projects specific to the HR area * Experience delivering HR initiatives within a changing work environment * Experience in public sector and commercial/semi\-state environments **EFFECTIVE PERFORMANCE INDICATORS** * People Management: (In regard to working as part of a team and stakeholder engagement) * Analysis \& Decision Making * Delivery of Results * Interpersonal \& Communication Skills * Specialist Knowledge, Expertise and Self\-Development **SELECTION PROCESS** **The Selection Process may include:** 1. Shortlisting of candidates, based on the information contained in their application. 2. A competitive preliminary interview which may include a presentation. 3. Psychometric testing. 4. Work sample/role play/media exercise, and any other tests or exercises that may be deemed appropriate. 5. A second competitive interview which may include a presentation. 6. Garda Vetting **Salary Range** The salary range for this role is €36,225 to €71,151 (Incremental Scale). **Probationary Period** The position is subject to a probationary period of 6 months from the date of actual commencement and is subject to the provision of satisfactory performance during that period. **Hours of Attendance** Hours of attendance will be fixed from time to time but will amount to not less than 41 hours gross (36 hours net) per week. The successful candidate will be required to work additional hours from time to time as may be reasonable and necessary for the proper performance of their duties subject to the limits set down in the working time regulations. **Annual Leave** Annual Leave excluding public holidays is 23 days per annum rising to 24 days after 5 years’ service, 26 days per annum after 10 years’ service, 27 days per annum after 12 years’ service and 28 days per annum after 15 years’ service. **Pension** The successful candidate will on date of joining become a member of the Irish Aviation Authority pension scheme details of which will be communicated to the successful candidate on offer of appointment. **Other Benefits** * Access to cycle to work scheme * Tax free Bus/ Luas/ Rail to assist with travel costs to and from work * Access to Employee Assistance Programme * Access to IAA health and wellbeing initiatives * Learning and Development opportunities **Location** The IAA is based in the Times Building, 11\-12 D'Olier Street, Dublin 2 near Trinity College. We are convenient to all Luas lines; several Dublin Bus routes and are about 5 minutes from Tara Street Railway Station. We are also just minutes from O’Connell Street and St. Stephen’s Green. **Hybrid working arrangements may be agreed in line with IAA Flexible Working policy.** **Application** Applications must consist of the following to be considered for shortlisting: * A letter of motivation (max. two A4 pages) outlining why you wish to be considered for the post and where you believe your skills and experience meet the requirements of the position * A comprehensive CV **Complete applications including a CV and letter of motivation** should be received no later than 5pm Wednesday 27th May 2026\. **Eligibility** Candidates should note that eligibility to compete is open to citizens of the European Economic Area (EEA), Switzerland, the UK and those with an IRP with stamp 4\. To qualify candidates must be citizens of the EEA, Switzerland, the UK or have an IRP with stamp 4 on the date of application. **The IAA are not in the position to offer sponsorship for this role.** The IAA promotes equity, diversity, and inclusion and welcomes applications from people from diverse backgrounds and under\-represented groups including, but not limited to, ethnic minority and people with disabilities. IAA firmly believes that such diversity enriches our workplace culture and drives innovation. If you have a disability or need any specific accommodations during the application or selection process, please let us know. We are committed to providing reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have an equal opportunity to participate fully in the recruitment process. You can contact us at human.resources@iaa.ie.

title: IPG Reporting Analyst
description: IPG Reporting Analyst \- Dublin \- Hybrid ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. **The Role** The Investigator Payments Group (IPG) is the investigator payments function for ICON Clinical Research. The department role is to ensure timely, accurate and efficient execution of investigator payments \& to ensure all payments are carried out in accordance with ICON policies and procedures. **The Analyst Role Is To** * Support reporting requirements ensuring compliance with any Healthcare reporting legislation * Support operational reporting for IPG team **Role Responsibilities Include** * Recognise, exemplify and adhere to ICON's values which centre around our commitment to People, Clients and Performance. * Complete reports and tracking in a timely and consistent manner with concise, accurate information in various systems and tracking tools. * Ensure proper documentation and record tracking of meetings, decisions, interventions and escalations. * Liaise with customers to determine report requirements. * Interrogate \& analyse data and prepare relevant metrics. Accurate analysis with limited supervision is expected. * Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines, where necessary * Become proficient in systems, tools and ICON’s applications to efficiently and accurately analyse data. * Perform other reasonably related duties, analysis and special projects as assigned by management team. * As a member of staff, the employee is expected to embrace and contribute to our culture of process improvement with a focus on streamlining our processes adding value to our business and meeting client needs. **To be successful in the role, you will ideally have** * Bachelor’s degree in finance, data analytics, business administration, or a related field * Prior relevant experience related to data analysis and production of reports is essential * Advanced excel skills are essential (Pivot tables, lookups, etc.) * Demonstrate a logical approach to analysis with a questioning attitude * Numerate with some financial understanding * Excellent working knowledge of MS office package * Excellent communication (written and oral), organisational and customer service skills are essential * Self\-starter who can work with minimal supervision The role is only open to candidates Irish \& EU citizens or candidates with a minimum of a Stamp 4 visa. We cannot consider candidates with Stamp 1, 2,1G etc for this particular role. **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: IT & Systems Administrator
description: Fine Gael is seeking an IT \& Systems Administrator to manage and support the organisation’s IT systems and digital infrastructure. This role is responsible for ensuring the smooth day\-to\-day operation of IT services, supporting staff, and maintaining secure and efficient systems. The role is based in Dublin 2 and has a strong focus on Microsoft 365 and cloud\-based tools. There is also an opportunity to support improvements in how data is structured and used across systems, including dashboard and reporting work where appropriate. It is a role that will be of particular interest to individuals who are interested in and curious about how technology can be used to support political activity. The role offers a competitive salary, career progression opportunities and generous annual leave allowance. **Main Duties \& Responsibilities** *IT Systems \& Support* * Manage and administer Microsoft 365 (users, licenses, Teams, SharePoint, Exchange). * Provide day\-to\-day IT support to staff (hardware, software and access issues). * Manage onboarding and offboarding of users (accounts, permissions and devices). * Maintain and support laptops, printers and other equipment. * Troubleshoot system issues and liaise with external IT providers were required * Support the management and maintenance of the Fine Gael website. * Provide IT support for Party events, including Ard Fheis, conferences and online meetings, ensuring systems and technology run smoothly. *Security \& Compliance* * Maintain IT security including user access controls, MFA and device security. * Support compliance with GDPR and internal data protection policies. * Maintain backups and support disaster recovery processes. * Ensure appropriate controls are in place for handling sensitive data. *Systems \& Process Improvement* * Support the development and improvement of internal systems and workflows. * Assist in automating processes where possible. * Work with teams to ensure systems are used effectively. * Stay up to date with emerging technologies and identify opportunities to improve systems, efficiency and user experience across the organisation. *Data \& Reporting Support* * Support the development and maintenance of dashboards (e.g. Power BI). * Assist in structuring data across systems to enable reporting. * Work with teams to improve data quality and consistency. * Provide basic reporting support where required. **Skills** * Strong experience administering Microsoft 365\. * Experience providing end\-user IT support. * Good understanding of IT security and data protection. * Experience working with cloud\-based systems. * Ability to manage external vendors and service providers. * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work independently. * Experience with Power BI and Power Automate (or similar tools), along with an understanding of data structures and reporting. **Note:** * Full Time, Fixed term 2\-year Contract . * Closing Date, 5pm Friday 29th May 2026

title: IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** We are seeking a skilled and proactive IT Operations Engineer to join our team. In this critical role, you will be instrumental in delivering IT support to Distilled’s Employees enabling a seamless hybrid working environment and ensuring everyone has the tools and support needed to do their best work, while maintaining the smooth operation and continuous improvement of our internal IT systems. You'll leverage your expertise to resolve complex technical challenges, contribute to strategic IT initiatives and work with a diverse range of cutting\-edge technologies that drive our company's success. This is an exciting opportunity to deepen your technical knowledge, enhance our operational efficiency, and play a key role in scaling our IT environment. **Location:** This role requires working from our Dublin office 4 days per week. **What will I be doing** As an IT Operations Engineer, you, along with the existing ITOps team, will be responsible for a wide range of technical operations, ensuring the delivery of dependable and resilient IT services. The ideal candidate can balance reactive support—managing urgent incoming issues—with proactive work such as scheduled maintenance and strategic improvements, while consistently looking for opportunities to optimize systems \& processes and to ensure that our employees have the tools to do their best work. * **IT Support \& Service Delivery:** Deliver comprehensive remote and onsite IT support through the ticketing system for approximately 250 employees across multiple locations and countries, with a strong focus on providing an excellent service experience for all users. Responsibilities include troubleshooting hardware, software, and system issues, managing equipment lifecycle and procurement, and overseeing the user lifecycle, including onboarding and offboarding. The role also requires a proactive approach to anticipating user needs and continuously improving the support experience. * **Infrastructure Administration:** Administer, monitor and optimize critical IT infrastructure, including networking, firewalls, video conferencing and AV systems, ensuring performance, security and high availability. Manage and secure remote devices via Jamf, Intune and Google Workspace * **Identity \& Access Management:** Manage user accounts and permissions within Okta/Active Directory, encompassing security policy and MFA administration. Optimise the IT aspects of employee onboarding and offboarding processes. * **Operational Excellence \& Documentation:** Drive continuous improvement initiatives for IT operations. Create and maintain comprehensive IT documentation. Co\-manage IT asset inventory and refine escalation processes. * **Project Contribution \& Event Support:** Contribute to the planning and implementation phases of IT projects with a focus on innovation, automation and security. Provide technical support for company\-wide initiatives and events. **What skills and experience do I need** * At least 3 years experience in an IT Operations or System Administration role providing hands on support for employees. * Extensive enterprise experience administering and troubleshooting macOS, Windows, and mobile devices, with strong expertise in MDM solutions including Intune, Jamf and Google Workspace. * Proven expertise in administering and supporting Google Workspace or Office 365\. * Hands\-on experience with administering Cloud Computing platforms (AWS/Azure/ Google Cloud), including IaaS and PaaS components. * Experience in managing and troubleshooting enterprise\-level Video Conferencing systems. * Solid understanding and practical experience with Networking concepts (VPNs, Firewalls, Wi\-Fi, VLANs, routing, switching). * Experience with Identity Management platforms (e.g., Okta, Azure AD Identity Protection, SSO integration). * GDPR and information security focused. * A proactive, adaptable professional with outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, as well as a technical and problem\-solving mindset. **Advantage if you have:** * Experience with scripting languages for automation and system administration. * Familiarity with IT security best practices and incident response. * Industry recognised credentials: Microsoft Azure/Endpoint/SecOps, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Okta Professional/Administrator, Jamf 100/170/300 , Associate Google Workspace Administrator, Comptia Security\+, ITIL Foundation. * Experience in upcoming AI trends in predictive security, agentic AI for automation and specialized IT service management (ITSM) to handle AI\-driven threats **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together** . **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen** . We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together**. **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen**. We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: Implementation Specialist, Platform
description: **About Rippling** Rippling gives businesses one place to run HR, IT, and Finance. It brings together all of the workforce systems that are normally scattered across a company, like payroll, expenses, benefits, and computers. For the first time ever, you can manage and automate every part of the employee lifecycle in a single system. Take onboarding, for example. With Rippling, you can hire a new employee anywhere in the world and set up their payroll, corporate card, computer, benefits, and even third\-party apps like Slack and Microsoft 365—all within 90 seconds. Based in San Francisco, CA, Rippling has raised $1\.4B\+ from the world’s top investors—including Kleiner Perkins, Founders Fund, Sequoia, Greenoaks, and Bedrock—and was named one of America's best startup employers by Forbes. We prioritize candidate safety. Please be aware that all official communication will only be sent from @Rippling.com addresses. **About The Role** Rippling’s Platform Specialist team is responsible for ensuring customers see incredible business transformations by maximizing the value of our core Platform\- including custom workflows, advanced reports/analytics, permission profiles, and approvals. Platform Specialist engagements are consultative and customized based on the customers’ unique needs/pain points. The ideal candidate is a customer advocate, product advocate, and natural problem solver. **This role specifically, is technical. A candidate who is strong in Excel/ SQL and is curious about technology and platforms will succeed.** What you will do * Ensure our customers are set up for success with our core platform: * + Discover our customers' business needs and priorities + Solution customer’s business needs/priorities via workflows, reports, permissions and approvals + Build custom workflows, formula fields (requires you to build queries in RQL), advanced reports, permission profiles, and approvals + Train customers on the core platform * Deliver to key KPI’s: project completions, CSAT, and adoption/exit gate adherence * Work cross\-functionally with CX, product, and marketing to maximize product adoption. * + Design and document new use cases for our platform. + Train our internal and external teams through webinars + Contribute to our knowledge base and serve as a subject matter expert on the Platform * Actively contribute to the development of new Platform features * + Coordinate with product and engineering teams to support new use cases. + Assist with user testing and beta programs for new features. What you will need * 2\-3 years of SaaS experience in professional services, implementation, or customer success * + An ability to build relationships with customers + An ability to manage a book of business and control the customer’s implementation experience + A curiosity for product/technology * Strong preference * + Experience writing your own SQL queries + Prior experience working at an HR/IT SaaS company * Ruthless prioritization and time management * Strong attention to detail and ability to solve complex, interdependent problems * Boundless energy to help other teams and your customers...all with a “can\-do” attitude! * Flexible to scaling and changing organizations and responsibilities * Experience working with product and engineering teams to build and launch new products or features

title: Information Technology Service Management Consultant
description: Join a forward\-thinking, internationally connected organisation operating at the forefront of a rapidly evolving, asset\-intensive industry. With a strong focus on digital transformation, this business is investing heavily in its technology landscape to support future growth. We are seeking an experienced IT Service \& Operations Manager to lead and enhance IT service delivery in a dynamic, multi\-vendor environment. This is a high\-impact role with strong visibility across the business. **Key Responsibilities** * Own and manage day\-to\-day IT service delivery and performance * Act as the primary escalation point for operational issues * Coordinate service desk, end\-user support, and workplace technologies * Manage third\-party vendors and ensure service levels are achieved * Oversee incident, problem, and change management processes * Support operational security, compliance, and control activities * Drive continuous improvement across IT services and processes. **Key Requirements** * Proven experience in IT service management or IT operations * Strong understanding of end\-user computing, infrastructure, and service environments * Experience managing vendors in multi\-provider environments * Ability to drive outcomes across cross\-functional teams * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Familiarity with ITSM tools and ITIL practices **What’s on Offer** * Competitive salary and performance\-related bonus * Hybrid working with strong work\-life balance * Opportunity to shape and improve IT service delivery at scale * Exposure to international teams and modern technology environments * A collaborative, supportive culture focused on continuous improvement **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: My Dublin City Centre based client is recruiting for an IT Support Engineer (Level 2\) to join a growing team. This is a permanent role and will be fully onsite. The IT Support Engineer will support and maintain the firm’s Microsoft\-based IT environment. The role involves providing advanced technical support across Microsoft 365, Azure, desktop systems, networking, and end\-user services in a fast\-paced professional environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide Level 2 support for desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and remote users. * Support Microsoft 365 services including Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Exchange Online. * Administer Azure / Entra ID, Intune, MFA, and user access management. * Troubleshoot Windows 10/11, networking, VPN, printer, and hardware issues. * Assist with server support, Active Directory, patching, and backups. * Support cybersecurity, endpoint protection, and compliance standards. * Participate in IT projects, upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives. **Required Skills** * Experience supporting Microsoft 365 and Azure environments. * Strong knowledge of Windows desktop support and troubleshooting. * Experience with Intune / Endpoint Manager and Entra ID. * Understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, VPN, TCP/IP). * Excellent communication and customer service skills. * Experience in a legal or professional services environment desirable. **Qualifications** * IT qualification or equivalent experience. * Microsoft certifications advantageous.

description: Role \- IT Support Engineer \- SCCM Intune Dublin, Ireland **Responsibilities:** * Respond to queries raised via a national telephone number, email, and through Service Manager * Take ownership, troubleshoot, and seek to resolve to completion all reported IT issues * Liaise/escalate calls to the National IT teams and Regional Managers as appropriate * Support the management of our IT fixed assets. * Manage software installations on endpoints using a combination of Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager * and local installations, on a Windows 10 Enterprise Platform * Assist role.

title: Inside Sales Executive - Airbnb
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. **About The Inside Sales Executive:** MarketStar is looking for an Inside Sales Executive for an exciting new project. In this role you will be identifying and recruiting hosts for our client's growing business. The perfect candidate will possess a deep knowledge of the client's platform and exceptional networking abilities. Your responsibilities will include researching prospects, initiating contact, and closing new business via phone and video conference. This is B2B sales with SME and Sole Trader and short sales cycles of up to 1 week. **About Our Client:** Our client is one of the largest players in the hospitality space, acting as a broker for 250 million happy customer transactions every year and turning over more than $10Billion in revenue in 2024\. This a household brand in the top 1,000 organisations in terms of revenue, listed as a Forbes Global 2000 company and featured in the TIME World’s Best Companies of 2024\. The potential in this project for a successful candidate is truly substantial! **Location:** Leopardstown, Dublin 18 \- Onsite (Mon \- Fri) **What will you do?** * Research and identify potential new customers * Reach out to prospects via email, phone, or social media * Conduct initial conversations to gauge interest and suitability * Manage ongoing communications until a prospect becomes a customer * Manage a book of prospects * Maintain accurate information within CRM systems and gdocs **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * 1 year of experience in recruitment, retail sales, cold calling, insurance sales, telecoms or utilities sales, general transactional B2C or B2C sales or B2B sales development * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. * Strong organizational and time\-management skills. * Proactive and self\-motivated with a results\-driven approach. * Flexibility to adapt to changing priorities and business needs. **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Employer Matched Pension Scheme \& Education reimbursement (after 6 months) * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €38,000 with an additional variable bonus component of €6,000 per annum. There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual who identifies as someone with a disability and require reasonable accommodation(s) to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at *people.success@marketstar.com* for assistance.

title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Dublin,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Dublin, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions** . The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time** , and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of the time, depending on overall activity requirements) **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 75% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Intermediary Support Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for almost 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has arisen within the support function of Davy Select for an Intermediary Support Associate. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Ensure Brokers requirements are understood and met in terms of turnaround and accuracy and that Brokers are consistently provided with excellent customer service; * Manage the opening of new accounts and account queries; * Liaise with internal teams within Davy Operations; * Update broker and client pipelines and Management Information; * Provide general administrative support to the Davy Select Account Managers; * Actively seek to add value to processes by introducing improvements based upon Broker \& Client feedback; * Develop a thorough working knowledge of pension and investments and the appropriate product knowledge; * Involvement in ad hoc projects \& business development as required. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role. **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * A Business/Finance degree or equivalent; * QFA qualification or willingness to complete; * Knowledge of settlements and operations; * A proven record in reconciliation and control; * Advanced knowledge of Word, Excel and Outlook; * Good numeric skills; * Customer service focused with a record of providing excellent customer service; * Accuracy and attention to detail; * Excellent organisational skills; * Ability to prioritise and work to deadlines; * A positive attitude, strong work ethic, flexible outlook and a high level of initiative and enthusiasm; **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Investment Analyst - Global Fund
description: **Job Title: Investment Analyst** **Sector: Global Investment Firm** **Location: Dublin \- Hybrid** **Salary: Base negotiable plus exceptional bonus opportunity and benefits** **Our Client** Our client are a Global Investment Firm. They have a track\-record of delivering exceptional results and have an excellent reputation globally. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a driven and ambitious individual who is seeking to advance their career in an exciting industry. You will be part of an investment team that has exposure to all projects, large scale, niche, and highly specialised ventures. title: Join the Jentic Talent Community
description: **Description** **About Jentic** At Jentic, we’re on a mission to build the core integration layer for AI, enabling developers to connect their AI systems to the world’s APIs easily and securely. We’re creating a critical missing piece of the future AI tech stack — middleware designed from the ground up for the AI era. We’re not just building an AI\-facing product, but an AI\-native company. We’re building a small highly\-productive team, in which everyone is amplified with AI, and where everyone shares personal and financial ownership in our mission. Joining Jentic means shaping the future of software in a team that is built for the future. **What We’re Building** Jentic is the standalone integration layer that connects AI agents with other systems and each other. Built by developers, for developers, our platform simplifies and accelerates API integration, increasing AI agent reliability, while solving security and access control challenges. **Requirements** **About Our Jentic Talent Community** Thank you for your interest in Jentic. We may not have a position right now that currently matches to your experience and expertise, however we would love if you shared your CV with us so we can get in touch with you about future roles when the time is right. Please note that being part of our Jentic Talent Community does not mean that you are actively applying for a role with us. It means that you'll be in our talent list so that when the right role comes up, we are able to contact you. **Benefits** **Why Join Us Now?** * Be a pioneer: Join an exceptionally experienced founding team at a critical stage in Jentic’s journey. Early team members will play a foundational role in the company’s growth and direction. * Mark your mark: Help define the core architecture of the future AI stack and make your mark during this unprecedented platform shift to AI. * Developer\-focused mission: Build tools that solve real\-world problems developers face when integrating AI agents. * AI\-native culture: Learn how to leverage AI to multiply your effectiveness and help create an organisation that reflects what businesses will look like in the years to come. **Take a First Class Ticket on the AI Train** This is your chance to work on a critical missing piece of the future of AI in a startup led by seasoned founders with a track record of success. Join us in building the essential middleware for AI, and help shape the future of software during the most transformative shift in decades. Ready to get on board? Apply now and let’s build the future together. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** Jentic is an equal opportunity employer, and we value diversity in the workplace. We are committed to providing an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates. We don't just tolerate differences—we celebrate them. Our strength comes from perspectives that challenge the status quo.

title: Junior Full Stack Engineer
description: **EXL (NASDAQ: EXLS)** is a global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes and unlock growth with speed. EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com. **Role Title:** Full Stack Engineer (Junior level) **BU/Segment:** Digital **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * Design, develop, and maintain full\-stack applications using Python (backend) and React.js (frontend) * Develop APIs and microservices to support AI agent communications and data exchange * Implement real\-time features for agent monitoring, logging, and performance tracking * Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues across the full stack * Work closely with product managers, designers, and other engineers to deliver high\-quality solutions * Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code that prioritizes security and data protection. * Maintain high software quality standards within the team by adhering to best practices. Identify and encourage areas for growth and improvement within the group. * Work in an agile delivery/ DevOps methodology to deliver enterprise\-grade production implementation in iterative sprints. * Explore and leverage AI tools to help improve innovation, efficiency and quality across the SDLC. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * Minimum 1\+ Years of experience in application development \& support. * Experience in any programming language. * Experience with any frontend technology like react.js etc. * Understanding of any of the cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP), * Knowledge of algorithms, design patterns, and fundamental computer science concepts \& data structures * Experience with CI/CD pipelines **Good to have (optional)** * Experience with AI/ML libraries such as LangChain, OpenAI API, Bedrock, or similar framework * Understanding of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure\-as\-code (Terraform, CDK). **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * Strong communication skills and ability to work with various stakeholders across various geographies * Master's or bachelor's degree, preferably from an Engineering Background * Be a self\-motivated, curious, and productive under high pressure **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. We offer a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities. We also support a range of learning initiatives that allow our employees to build on their existing skills and knowledge. From online courses to seminars and workshops, our employees have the opportunity to enhance their skills and stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies. * As an Equal Opportunity Employer, **EXL** is committed to diversity. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Junior Product Development Engineer
description: The successful candidate will join the R\&D team to take responsibility for complex LV distribution projects in the electrical power industry. You will be required to commit considerable effort to truly understand our customer’s systems and processes and identify ways to add value. **Responsibilities will include:** * Reporting to the R\&D Manager, the Electrical Design Engineer will work as part of the ECAD team, ensuring deliverables required by the project are developed in line with company protocols and procedures. * Design of critical power distribution systems, PLC based control panels and energy * monitoring systems. * Assemble document packages and develop drawing sets for review * Application of relevant international design standards to all design projects. (e.g IEC * 61439, UL 891\) * Communicate progress on design plan and design deliverables to the R\&D Manager * Submitting designs and BOM’s for review and any other design deliverable documentation for review approval * Coordinating with the assigned project lead, ensuring alignment on deliverables and timelines and any running changes during the design phase of a project. * Supporting continuous improvement \& development of the R\&D Team processes and Controls **Requirements:** * Be degree qualified in Electrical Engineering or similar with minimum 3 years experience in similar environment. * Previous experience designing low voltage power distribution products. * Demonstrated success working to prescribed standards. * Experience with Solid Works Electrical or similar electrical design software. * Experience of Building Management Systems would be advantageous, but not essential. * Have excellent communication skills with a professional approach to work and sharp eye for detail. * Good team player, but capable of working independently. * Driving License and Access to a vehicle * Willingness to travel if required. **Why Hanley Energy:** * **Launchpad for Your Career:** Joining Hanley Energy means stepping into a world of endless opportunities. Gain hands\-on experience that will set the stage for a prosperous career. * **Nurturing Environment:** You won’t just be a part of a team; you’ll be part of a supportive family where your growth is celebrated and nurtured. * **Shape Our Future:** At Hanley Energy, your voice matters. Contribute your ideas and be a driving force behind our continued growth and success. * **Competitive Package:** Enjoy a well\-deserved competitive salary and benefits package that acknowledges your dedication and hard work. **Company Benefits:** * Salary with excellent career development opportunities * Company Healthcare Plan * Company Pension Scheme * Death in Service Life Insurance * Employee Assistance Programme \& Wellbeing Programme * Academic assistance \& Structured In\-house training * Hanley Energy Affinity Scheme * Free parking at all locations * Hybrid Work Model where applicable **Work Location:** Dundalk

title: Junior Project Coordinator
